title: KAS host callbacks gated by cargo feature, not bound engine — implement ADR-0001 capability accessors or amend the ADR
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review (SoC lens). In a --features kas build running the V2 engine, KiroClient still answers kiro/auth/getAccessToken with the real token and answers fs/* and terminal/* callbacks if the subprocess sends them (client.rs:192-281) — despite V2Engine advertising no such capabilities, and despite ADR-0001's design intent that these are surfaces 'KAS implements and v2 does not.' The cargo feature quietly replaced the bound engine as the availability gate for host callbacks.

ADR-0001's consequences also promise the capability sub-trait mechanism ('the accessor pattern is introduced in KAS-1, where AuthResponder gets a real implementation and consumer') — no AuthResponder trait exists anywhere; auth landed as a static feature-gated handler, fs/terminal as feature-gated KiroClient overrides. The ADR text is stale.

Practical risk is LOW today (the subprocess is kiro-cli; v2 never sends these callbacks — the code says so at client.rs:23-24,180). But the next capability (KAS-7 hooks, _kiro/safety/*, cyril-3ald) will copy whatever precedent exists, so resolve before then. DISTINCT from cyril-g9vt: that issue is about the ADR-0004 stage-interception seam; this one is engine-vs-feature gating of responder availability.

Cheapest fix short of sub-traits: have the responders consult the bound engine (KiroClient already holds Rc<dyn Engine>) before answering — refuse with method-not-found when the engine does not advertise the capability. Either way, amend ADR-0001's consequences to match what shipped.
acceptance_criteria: A kas-feature build running the v2 engine refuses getAccessToken / fs/* / terminal/* (method-not-found or equivalent), test-fenced; OR ADR-0001 is amended to document feature-level gating as the accepted design with the trade-off stated. Stale AuthResponder consequence text corrected in either outcome.

title: KAS host-io: translate WSL-internal paths (/home, /tmp) for a Windows host
description:
cyril's platform::path::wsl_to_win only translates /mnt/<drive> mount paths (/mnt/c/... -> C:\...). WSL-internal paths (/home/<user>, /tmp, /root) pass through unchanged. On a Windows host running `wsl kiro-cli acp`, a KAS workspace under the WSL-native filesystem makes fs/{read,write}_text_file callbacks carry /home/... paths.

After cyril-7bdu's to_native_checked fix these now fail HONESTLY as -32603 NotFound (has_root() accepts them, to_native leaves them unchanged, tokio::fs cannot open a WSL ext4 path from the Windows host) instead of a misleading 'must be absolute' -32602 -- but they still do not RESOLVE. Full Windows+KAS support for WSL-native workspaces needs \\wsl$\<distro>\... (or \\wsl.localhost\...) translation in platform::path.

Latent today: KAS is Linux-only in practice; /mnt/<drive> workspaces already work after the fix. Discovered during max-effort review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: wsl_to_win maps a WSL-internal path (e.g. /home/u/p/x.txt) to a \\wsl$\<distro>\... UNC path on Windows (no-op on Linux); round-trip + json-translation tests; distro-name resolution decided ($WSL_DISTRO_NAME / config); a KAS fs read+write against a WSL-native workspace succeeds on a Windows host.

title: Steering: KAS 0.8.0 adds _session/steer/clear (KAS-only; v2 lacks it) — adopt for queue-clear UX
description: kiro-cli 2.11.0 ships @kiro/agent 0.8.0 whose bundle adds _session/steer/clear alongside _session/steer (agent-side static diff vs 0.3.299; zero removals). The v2 Rust binary does NOT contain the full literal in 2.10.0 or 2.11.0 (only an unchanged pooled '_session/steer/c…' fragment), so treat clear as KAS-only until live-probed. Relevant to K1 steering: gives cyril a way to clear a queued steer instead of only adding. Runtime behavior unprobed — schema-accepted != functional; probe on KAS before building UX (send steer, then steer/clear, verify queue drains and no turn consumes the cleared steer).
acceptance_criteria: Live probe result recorded (KAS accepts/rejects + v2 -32601 confirmed); if functional, /steer clear or Esc-to-unqueue wired behind engine gate
notes: LIVE-PROBED 2026-07-02 — CONFIRMED FUNCTIONAL on KAS 0.8.0, genuinely absent on 0.3.299 (control: PersistenceClassification error). Contract: request {sessionId} -> {cleared:true, messageIds:[...]}; broadcasts session_info_update kind=steering_cleared {messageIds}; empty queue=no-op []; missing/unknown sessionId -> -32603 w/details. Does NOT abort the active turn (only drops queued steers; distinct from cancel); bumps steering epoch (drops concurrent/in-flight _session/steer append) + persists reload-safe boundary marker. Live lifecycle: steer {sessionId, message} -> {queued:true, messageId:'steer-<uuid>'} + steering_queued; clear returned that exact id + steering_cleared. steer param is 'message' (string) NOT 'content[]'. End-to-end model-omits-instruction NOT verified (turn 403'd on stale SSO token; credential synth declined as out-of-scope) — epoch/buffer/notify lifecycle conclusive. v2 gate: v2 binary lacks the literal; send optimistically, treat -32601/PersistenceClassification as engine-unsupported. Probes: experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-steer-clear-{sandbox,behavior}-2.11.0.py.

title: Bridge: engine death and prompt failure are invisible — prompt Err collapses to TurnCompleted(EndTurn); io-task death log-only; fail-stop BridgeDisconnected droppable
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review. Engine-agnostic bridge code, but KAS makes it material: the most likely real-world KAS failure (the node process crashing or wedging mid-turn) currently presents as an agent that quietly stopped answering.

(1) The off-loop prompt task maps conn.prompt() Err to StopReason::EndTurn and emits ONLY TurnCompleted — no BridgeError (bridge.rs:563-577). Directly violates CLAUDE.md 'Bridge errors must notify the App — logging alone is invisible.'
(2) The io pump's death is tracing::error! only (bridge.rs:395-399). The acp crate clears pending_responses when io ends (rpc.rs:78), so a mid-turn crash resolves the prompt as Err -> the user sees the turn just end with zero output and zero explanation. The loop stays alive against the dead connection: every subsequent SendPrompt errors fast and again produces a silent instant TurnCompleted — the session looks idle-and-ignoring-me until the user happens to run /new (whose arm DOES emit BridgeDisconnected).
(3) This also swallows the auth responder's actionable errors: auth.rs:160-163 returns 'kiro token expired; run kiro-cli login' TO KAS, not to the App — KAS fails the turn and the user gets silence.
(4) Sub-item: the fail-stop BridgeDisconnected uses try_send (bridge.rs:163-174) and is dropped with only a warn! if the notification channel is full — plausible exactly during a heavy streaming backlog, the moment a crash is most likely. Use a bounded send with timeout or a reserved slot.

NOTE: the turn_in_flight gate itself is safe (the Err arm still synthesizes TurnCompleted, so the gate always clears) — the problem is purely that the failure is mute.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the agent process mid-turn surfaces a visible error to the user (BridgeError and/or BridgeDisconnected) in the same turn; a SendPrompt against a dead connection produces an actionable error message, not a silent TurnCompleted; the fail-stop disconnect signal cannot be dropped by a full channel; auth-stale turn failures reach the user with the actionable login hint.

title: K1b — Queue-steering TUI UX
description: User-facing steering UX on top of K1a plumbing. Enter-while-busy: when session.status()==Busy and input is non-slash text, route submit to SteerSession instead of session/prompt (idle unchanged; also fixes the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug). Local echo in the transcript (steer: <msg> — queued), updated when steering_consumed arrives. Toolbar chip while >=1 steer queued, cleared on consumed/cleared/turn-end. /steer command for the idle path. Advisory UI copy (the agent weighs the steer and can decline).
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17. Started immediately after K1a merged.

title: Enter-while-busy silently drops the user's message (regression from cyril-84ca off-loop guard)
description:
Regression introduced by cyril-84ca (PR #22): pressing Enter (non-slash text) while session.status()==Busy silently drops the user's message.

ROOT CAUSE: app.rs submit_input() (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:640) has NO busy guard. On Enter it take_input()s (clears the box), add_user_message()s (commits the text to the visible chat), set_status(Busy)+set_activity(Sending), then sends BridgeCommand::SendPrompt. After cyril-84ca the bridge's one-turn guard rejects a second SendPrompt mid-turn with BridgeError{operation:'prompt', message:'a turn is already in progress'} (bridge.rs SendPrompt guard). So the message is committed to chat and the input cleared, but it is NEVER sent to the agent — only an error system message appears.

BEHAVIOR CHANGE: pre-cyril-84ca the blocked command loop QUEUED the second SendPrompt and ran it as a sequential second turn after the first completed (message eventually sent). Post-cyril-84ca it is rejected (message lost). Live on main in the window between cyril-84ca merging and cyril-bm1j (K1b) landing — cyril-84ca blocks cyril-bm1j, so this PR merges first.

SECOND AFFECTED PATH: dispatch_code_command's Prompt branch (app.rs:1043) programmatically emits SendPrompt with no busy check; a /code-triggered prompt arriving mid-turn is rejected the same way, and its already-committed user text is never sent.

UI INCOHERENCE: UiState's BridgeError handler (crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs:638) only add_system_message()s — it does NOT clear activity. So set_activity(Sending) from the rejected submit regresses the spinner from Streaming/ToolRunning back to 'Sending', the user sees 'prompt failed: a turn is already in progress', and then turn 1 keeps streaming — a sequence that did not occur pre-cyril-84ca.

PROPER FIX: this IS 'the latent second-prompt-mid-turn bug' that cyril-bm1j (K1b) is designed to fix by routing Enter-while-busy to SteerSession when status==Busy and input is non-slash. If the pre-K1b window matters, a minimal interim guard in submit_input (don't commit/clear + show a 'turn in progress' hint, or drop the second send before mutating UI) closes it without the full K1b UX.

Found by /code-review of PR #22.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting non-slash text while session.status()==Busy must not silently drop the message: either route it to SteerSession (K1b) or, at minimum, leave the input uncleared/uncommitted and surface a clear 'turn in progress' affordance, with no orphaned activity state (no spinner regression to Sending, no BridgeError-only dead end). The /code Prompt path (app.rs:1043) must likewise not lose its injected prompt mid-turn. Regression test: with a Busy session, submit Enter and assert the message is not lost and activity/status stay consistent.

title: KAS-1 follow-up: auto-refresh kiro token file on stale before getAccessToken reply (wrapper mode)
description: KAS-1 Part B (cyril-evwh) ships the wrapper auth responder reading ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json -> {accessToken,expiresAt,profileArn}. In WRAPPER mode KAS injects --auth=acp-callback and stops self-refreshing the file, so a STALE token (expiresAt <= now+180s) would be a known-bad reply. KAS-1 returns an actionable Err on stale (user re-runs kiro-cli login). THIS = automate it: on stale, trigger kiro-cli's own refresh (PROBE candidates: kiro-cli whoami/profile/user; OR the file-auth refresh path KAS itself uses) so the reply stays valid >now+3min WITHOUT reimplementing OIDC. Falsifier C9 (.cyril-evwh/design.md): force/await stale file, run candidate refresh, assert expiresAt advances >now+3min. DEFERRED from KAS-1: token was fresh during the build (staleness needs ~1h). is_stale() unit logic already lands in kas::auth; only the refresh trigger + live verification deferred here.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — four robustness requirements the implementation must state explicitly (the shell-out is inherently fragile: it relies on an undocumented side effect kiro could remove any release; probe-gating via falsifier C9 is the right mitigation and stays):
(1) tokio::process only, never std::process — the responder runs on the single-threaded bridge (auth.rs:52-54 non-blocking rationale applies doubly to spawning a process).
(2) A timeout — kiro-cli can hang on network.
(3) A single-flight guard so concurrent getAccessToken requests don't spawn N refresh processes.
(4) NEVER trust the exit code — re-read and re-validate via the existing read_token_file + is_stale after the refresh attempt; treat the shell-out purely as a best-effort trigger.
The safe fallback (actionable -32000 Err on stale, auth.rs:159-164) stays load-bearing: refresh failure degrades to today's behavior, never a known-bad reply.

title: dispatch_code_command Prompt path drops the message when busy (second cyril-2vcc path)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a SECOND instance of the cyril-2vcc bug class, outside the K1b diff so deferred. crates/cyril/src/app.rs dispatch_code_command's CodeCommandResponse::Prompt arm (app.rs:1152-1171) add_user_message()s + set_activity(Sending) + emits BridgeCommand::SendPrompt with NO busy guard. If a /code response yields a Prompt while session.status()==Busy, the post-cyril-84ca one-turn guard rejects the second SendPrompt with BridgeError -> the injected prompt is committed to chat but never sent, and activity regresses to Sending. cyril-2vcc fixed only submit_input (Enter-while-busy) via classify_submit->dispatch_steer; this /code prompt-injection path was not covered. FIX: gate this path on busy too (advisory or defer), mirroring the submit_input fix. Reachable when a user triggers a /code prompt-injection mid-turn.
design:
Falsifiable design (gilfoyle): .cyril-8ej2/falsifiable-design.md. Cheapest falsifier RUN + PASSED (busy+Prompt currently emits SendPrompt+UserText+Sending — bug confirmed).

DECISION: busy-case = advisory + drop (zero bridge commands, commit nothing, one System advisory), NOT steer — because dispatch_code_command is sync (steer path is async) and the issue specifies advisory/defer. Scope = Busy only, matching classify_submit.

7 claims, each with an independent unit-test falsifier + named buggy impl (non-vacuity) + a distinct regression fence:
1 Active+Prompt unchanged (1 SendPrompt, UserText, Sending) — extend dispatch_code_prompt_returns_deferred_command
2 Busy+Prompt emits zero commands — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_drops_no_send
3 Busy+Prompt commits no UserText — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_commits_no_user_message
4 Busy+Prompt doesn't set Sending — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_leaves_activity
5 Busy+Prompt adds System advisory — dispatch_code_prompt_busy_advises
6 no-session+Prompt unchanged — dispatch_code_prompt_no_session_shows_error (existing)
7 guard scoped to Prompt arm: Panel opens when Busy — dispatch_code_panel_opens_when_busy

Additive change (adds a guard; removes no invariant). Negative space: doesn't steer, doesn't guard Compacting/Initializing/Disconnected, doesn't queue/retry, doesn't touch Panel/Unknown/success-false, doesn't go async.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED on branch fix/cyril-8ej2-code-prompt-busy-guard, commit 1f44a2d. All gilfoyle gates green.
- Fix: runtime busy-guard in dispatch_code_command Prompt arm (crates/cyril/src/app.rs) — Busy => advise + drop (0 commands, no UserText, activity untouched). Scope = Busy only (matches classify_submit). Runtime check, not debug_assert (load-bearing for correctness in release).
- Fences (7, all pass): dispatch_code_prompt_{busy_drops_no_send, busy_commits_no_user_message, busy_leaves_activity, busy_advises}, dispatch_code_panel_opens_when_busy, dispatch_code_prompt_returns_deferred_command (extended: asserts UserText), dispatch_code_prompt_no_session_shows_error.
- RED->GREEN verified (4 busy fences failed pre-guard). Full workspace: 770 tests pass, clippy -D warnings clean, fmt --check clean.
- Artifacts: .cyril-8ej2/{falsifiable-design,budgeted-plan}.md.
Leave open pending review/merge (close per the project's PR-close pattern). NOT pushed.

title: KAS-2a follow-up: turn-seq dedup so a stale TurnCompleted can't clear a later same-session turn
description:
Slice 3 of cyril-j16p dedupes the double TurnCompleted (turn_end notification + prompt response) with a 'drop when turn_in_flight is None' guard in the bridge loop observer (bridge.rs:1370). Correct for the common case but has a residual: turn A's late prompt-response TurnCompleted, arriving AFTER turn_end cleared the flag AND a new same-session turn B started, would match B's turn_in_flight (session id is not per-turn unique) and wrongly clear it. Fix: give TurnCompleted a monotonic per-turn id (bridge.rs:562 already anticipates 'a turn id field to TurnCompleted'); the observer clears/forwards only on an id match. Low-risk for the KAS-2a walking skeleton (narrow window, trivial turns); deferred from cyril-j16p Slice 3. Ref ROADMAP:KAS-2a.

CROSS-SESSION DIMENSION (independently surfaced by all 5 /code-review max finders, 2026-06-29 — strong observability signal): the same turn_in_flight: Option<SessionId> gate is also session-blind. The observer (bridge.rs:1385) matches TurnCompleted by variant only and never compares routed.session_id. A non-in-flight session's turn_end -> TurnCompleted (e.g. a future KAS spec/secondary/parallel-turn session, per docs/kiro-2.7.1-wire-audit) would clear the MAIN turn's gate (premature un-busy + early SendPrompt accept), or be dropped when the gate is None. NOT reachable in KAS-2a (single session; KAS subagents are tool_calls with no per-session turn_end). The monotonic per-turn-id fix above ALSO resolves this, provided the id is globally unique across (session, turn). CONSTRAINT for the fixer: the off-loop prompt-response TurnCompleted is sent as RoutedNotification::global (session_id None, event.rs:251), so a naive 'clear only when routed.session_id == turn_in_flight' fix WITHOUT a turn id would break v2 — the global signal never matches Some(main), freezing every v2 turn busy. The fix must key on a turn id, not a bare session id.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) HOLE in the turn-id fix as sketched: the bridge loop can stamp only the SYNTHESIZED prompt-response TurnCompleted; the KAS turn_end TurnCompleted is produced by the converter (convert/kas.rs:34) from a wire frame the bridge cannot stamp — a stale same-session turn_end still matches by session, so the residual survives for that signal. Either (a) probe whether session_info_update carries a native turn identifier, or (b) the observer tracks 'one duplicate still expected for the previous (session, seq)'. The variant likely needs Option<TurnId> with dual matching (id-match for synthesized, session-match for wire-scoped turn_end) — a legitimate Option-for-absent, not a sentinel. Make it a TurnId(u64) newtype per CLAUDE.md (mirror TerminalRegistry's saturating_add counter idiom, terminal_io.rs:106).
(2) Design JOINTLY with cyril-pnwb — same observer code (bridge.rs:1370-1393); turn identity and stop-reason precedence are one 'which signal is authoritative' question, not two.
(3) Cross-session nuance to fold in: a non-in-flight session's turn_end clears the bridge gate while the App routes the scoped TurnCompleted to a subagent stream — a SPLIT-BRAIN (bridge accepts a new prompt, main UI stays Busy) rather than the premature un-busy described. Same fix covers it.

title: K1b cross-turn steer echo/chip turn-end semantics unverified (needs backend probe)
description: Code-review (cyril-bm1j, xhigh) found a latent ambiguity in K1b's turn-end handling. TurnCompleted resets the chip (steering_queued=0, claim C9) but leaves any still-Queued SteerEcho in scrollback (negative-space #6). If the backend PERSISTS an end-of-turn-un-consumed steer and drains it on the NEXT turn (the idle-steer capture .k1b-steering/idle-steer-wire-capture.log shows steers survive the idle->turn boundary, suggesting it does), then: (a) the chip UNDER-counts a still-pending steer after turn-end, and (b) the next turn's SteeringConsumed FIFO-flips the stale prior-turn Queued echo. If instead the backend DROPS un-consumed steers at turn-end, the current reset is right but the echo should be finalized too. Both directions bake in an unverified assumption, so the fix was deferred. ACTION: probe whether a steer queued at end-of-turn (no further tool boundary) drains on the next turn; then either (1) stop resetting the chip at turn-end and let it drain naturally, or (2) finalize leftover Queued echoes at turn-end to match the reset. The cross-SESSION variant was already fixed (session_created_finalizes_prior_session_steer_echoes); this is the same mechanism across a TURN boundary within one session.
design:
Falsifiable design: .cyril-7z7u/falsifiable-design.md. Scope = OPTIMISTIC CHIP. Cheapest falsifier passed (claim 2 echo-flip preserved, consumed_flips_oldest_queued_echo green).

FIX (crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs only): make steering_queued optimistic, in lockstep with the echo, so invariant steering_queued == #SteerEcho{Queued}.
- add_steer_echo: +1 (NEW)
- SteeringConsumed: -1 + flip oldest Queued->Applied (kept)
- SteeringQueued (wire): no count change (was +1; REMOVED — avoid double-count)
- TurnCompleted: no reset (was =0; REMOVED — late steer's count/echo must survive)
- SteeringUnsupported: =0 (NEW — clear the optimistic count)
- SteeringCleared / SessionCreated: =0 (kept)

SUBTRACTIVE: removes "count==0 after TurnCompleted" (still-holds claim 3: drains via consumed) + "every increment has a wire match" (still-holds claim 4: wire queued must not double-count). echo logic (flip_queued_steer_echoes) UNCHANGED.

REWRITES 2 existing tests that lock the old model: turn_completed_resets_steering_queued (claim 3), steering_queue_mirror (claims 4/6/7). 9 claims total, each a state test fence.

Negative space: doesn't count observer-originated steers (cyril-8lfs); doesn't finalize echo at turn-end (rejects issue option b — probe shows late steer still pending).
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (worktree fix/cyril-7z7u-cross-turn-steer-semantics). All gilfoyle gates green.
- crates/cyril-ui/src/state.rs: steering_queued optimistic (add_steer_echo +1; SteeringConsumed -1; Cleared/Unsupported/SessionCreated =0; wire SteeringQueued no-op; TurnCompleted reset removed). Invariant: steering_queued == #SteerEcho{Queued}.
- 9 fences green incl. RED->GREEN rewrites of turn_completed_resets_steering_queued, steering_queue_mirror, steering_queued_exposed_via_tuistate_trait (locked the old model). Full workspace green, clippy -D clean, fmt clean.
- Discovered: SessionController.steering_depth = unread parallel wire counter, now divergent -> cyril-85py. Multi-observer steers -> cyril-8lfs.
Leave open pending review/merge.

title: KAS-2a — Walking skeleton: a plain KAS turn renders and ends
description:
The milestone that earns a demo: select KAS, authenticate, prompt, watch text + tool calls stream, turn completes, input is live again. The MINIMUM for a plain turn to render and end cleanly. Other _kiro/* surfaces (governance, mcp/status, powers, progressive_context) and the other session_info_update kinds all fire on a plain turn too, but cyril's existing unknown-variant debug! arm drops them gracefully — deferred to 2b-2d without hanging anything.

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-2a, docs/ROADMAP.md:165):
- Converter arm (engine-keyed, in the Engine-trait structure from KAS-0) for agent_message_chunk / agent_thought_chunk / tool_call / tool_call_update ONLY.
- session_info_update -> kind:'turn_end' mapped to cyril's turn-completion / busy-clear. THIS — not the session/prompt response — is the turn-completion signal under KAS. HANG-PROOFING: cyril's prompt-response await must be NON-BLOCKING under KAS — drive completion off turn_end, treat the prompt response as secondary/whenever-it-arrives, so a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton. Verify with existing probe-kas-* scripts (no new probe needed).
- Answer _kiro/terminal/shell_type ({sessionId} -> {shellType: bash|zsh|fish|powershell|sh}) — lightest host callback, called once at session setup; missing reply yields 'Shell: undefined' in the system prompt.
- Handle sess_*-prefixed session ids and per-run log dirs under ~/.kiro/logs/<ts>/.
- Defensive unknown-variant tolerance for everything else (one debug! line noting _kiro/* surfaces are intentionally dormant pre-2b).

CRITICAL REWORK (ROADMAP note, docs/ROADMAP.md:177): the off-loop busy-guard + TurnCompleted emission from PR #22 (cyril-84ca) must be reworked here. That PR emits TurnCompleted when conn.prompt().await resolves and keys the one-turn busy-guard off prompt_task.is_finished() — both encode 'prompt response == turn end', correct for v2 but FALSE for KAS (turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end (next prompt wrongly rejected 'a turn is already in progress') and TurnCompleted fires late/never — the exact hang the non-blocking criterion guards against. Fix: route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted through the engine's turn-end signal (KAS-0 turn-end detection in the Engine trait), not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: the SendPrompt guard + spawned prompt_task in bridge.rs.

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-2a (docs/ROADMAP.md:165); existing experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-*.py.
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine: select KAS, authenticate, send a prompt; agent text + tool calls stream and render, the turn completes, and input becomes live again.
* Turn completion is driven by session_info_update->turn_end, NOT the session/prompt response; the prompt-response await is non-blocking (a non-returning response cannot freeze the skeleton).
* The busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission are routed through the engine turn-end signal (not prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await); a second prompt after a logical turn-end is accepted, not rejected with 'a turn is already in progress'.
* _kiro/terminal/shell_type is answered (no 'Shell: undefined').
* sess_*-prefixed session ids handled; unknown _kiro/* variants produce a single debug! line, no hang.
* v1/v2 engine path unchanged.
notes:
2026-06-21 — The KAS-1 FREE PATH unblocks this demo without cyril-evwh's full auth responder. Direct-spawn `acp-server.js --transport=stdio` with no --auth authenticates from the SSO cache (after `kiro-cli login`): 0 _kiro/auth/getAccessToken calls across init + session/new + a tool turn (probe-kas-direct-spawn-2.8.1.py, 2.8.1). The cyril-evwh "blocks" dependency still holds for the production/wrapper path, but a demo is reachable sooner via the free path — worth weighing in triage.

Skeleton facts captured live (validate the KAS-2a acceptance criteria against these):
  - session/new returns sessionId "sess_..."; _meta.agentMode "vibe"; configOptions POPULATED (mode: vibe/spec/quick-spec/bug-fix/plan/autonomous/semantic_reviewer, autopilot on/off, contentCollection) — unlike v2 where configOptions is null (relevant to KAS-4 / cyril has no issue yet).
  - agentCapabilities._meta.kiro: checkpoints, sessionList, policyNotifications, extensionMethods [_kiro/knowledge, _kiro/codeIntelligence, _kiro/session/{context,compact,export,history}]; sessionCapabilities {fork, list} non-empty.
  - Turn-end rides session_info_update (~10x in one tiny turn); the session/prompt response also returned {stopReason: end_turn}. The NON-BLOCKING acceptance criterion (drive completion off turn_end, treat prompt response as secondary) can be validated on the free path today.
  - No agent->client callbacks on a no-tools turn (this config advertised no host capabilities). _kiro/terminal/shell_type did NOT fire here, and that is EXPECTED, not an open question: shell_type is gated on advertising terminal capability (acp-server.js resolveCapabilities: terminal === true). probe-kas-fs-terminal-host-2.7.1.py advertised terminal:true and saw shell_type fire once at session setup (also docs/kiro-2.7.1-wire-audit.md:344). So when wiring the KAS-2a shell_type responder, advertise terminal capability and answer once at setup; with no terminal cap it correctly never fires.

title: KAS-3 — Subagent rendering must handle dropped/empty/never-terminal results (upstream Kiro #9542)
description:
KAS subagent results can legitimately be empty, dropped, or never delivered — cyril's KAS-3 agent-subtask rendering (ROADMAP KAS-3; unfiled until now) must degrade gracefully. Surfaced by upstream Kiro bug #9542 (subagent "No result").

THREE upstream failure modes cyril will observe on the wire:
1. (v2) premature channel close: parent goes Idle before the subagent's `summary` is delivered -> stage result = "No result" (trace WARN: 'tool execution event for an agent not processing tools ... active_state: Idle, executing_subagents: {}').
2. (v3/KAS) null-response schema-validation fail: subagent hits max_tokens on long output -> response=null -> "No result". Intermittent, output-length-driven.
3. session-level channel corruption: a CANCELLED multi-stage `depends_on` pipeline leaves the multiplexer channel un-reset -> ALL subsequent subagent calls return "No result" until CLI restart.

cyril impact (cyril is an OBSERVER — can't fix upstream, must render robustly):
- Crew panel / SubagentUiState must NOT show a subagent as perpetually 'running' when its result is dropped/never-terminal. Treat a quiet subagent with no terminal result as a terminal 'no result / interrupted' state. On KAS the "No result" arrives as the parent's `orchestrate_subagent` tool_call_update content — render faithfully.
- HAZARD cyril can TRIGGER: Esc -> session/cancel during a KAS multi-stage subagent pipeline is exactly the Ctrl+C path that triggers upstream mode 3 (session-wide channel corruption). cyril should guard/warn, and if subagent results start coming back empty, surface a 'subagent channel may be wedged — /new to recover' hint.
- Repro/verify with the ACP wire recorder KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH=/tmp/acp-trace.jsonl ({ts,dir,msg} JSONL; lives in tui.js, confirmed 2.8.1).

Engine note: failure mode differs v2 vs v3. Related: cyril-9akh (notification-vs-response ordering race this validates), cyril-j16p (KAS-2a walking skeleton this builds on).
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — split acceptance criteria BY ENGINE (current wording conflates v2 and KAS paths):
- The 'Crew panel / SubagentUiState must not show perpetually running' line applies ONLY to v2 failure mode #1. On KAS those components receive nothing (no list_update); feeding KAS subtask data into SubagentTracker would violate the CLAUDE.md separate-path rule.
- KAS modes #2/#3 belong to the greenfield KAS-3 subtask path: a distinct grouper keyed on _meta.kiro.kind == 'agent-subtask' / agentSubtaskId, with _meta parsing in convert/kas.rs producing engine-neutral typed fields (UiState never reads raw _meta). Nothing in the crates handles agent-subtask yet — KAS-3 is greenfield, right moment to pin this.
- The never-terminal inference (quiet subagent with no terminal result -> render interrupted) and the wedge heuristic (empty results -> hint /new) are stateful inferences that live in that KAS-3 component, NOT SubagentTracker.
- The orchestrate_subagent tool_call_update rendering approach and the App-altitude Esc-hazard guard are architecturally clean as filed.

title: KAS-nhzw follow-up: overlay workspace kiro-cli settings onto global for _meta.kiro.settings
description: nhzw v1 marshals only the GLOBAL ~/.kiro/settings/cli.json into _meta.kiro.settings. kiro-cli also has a --workspace settings scope (the 'kiro-cli settings --workspace' flag exists), so a workspace cli.json should overlay global (workspace wins), matching kiro-cli precedence. v1 defers this because (a) the workspace cli.json path is unconfirmed (none exist on disk yet) and (b) global drives the observed behavior. Scope: confirm the path kiro-cli --workspace writes, read it if present, deep-merge over global before marshaling, add a claim+falsifier (workspace key overrides global). Discovered-from cyril-nhzw.

title: SessionController.context_usage not reset on SessionCreated/BridgeDisconnected (diverges from UiState)
description:
Latent divergence surfaced during the cyril-5et2 (KAS-2b) code review (PR #33), deliberately left unfixed to avoid scope creep into a file the PR didn't touch.

PR #33 added a SessionController arm for Notification::ContextBreakdownUpdated (crates/cyril-core/src/session.rs) so the documented 'context usage flows to both state machines' invariant (the context_usage_flows_to_both routing test) holds under KAS, where the scalar arrives only via that notification. UiState was also updated to RESET context_usage + context_breakdown on SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected (so a prior session's 'Context: N%'/bars don't leak across /new or linger on a dead connection).

SessionController's SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected arms do NOT clear self.context_usage, so after /new or disconnect SessionController retains the prior session's value while UiState clears it — the two state machines the App feeds (app.rs:307-308) now disagree on context lifecycle.

UNOBSERVABLE TODAY: nothing in non-test code reads SessionController::context_usage() (only the toolbar shows context, and it reads UiState). This is a trap for the next CommandContext consumer that reads session.context_usage() (e.g. a future /context- or /stats-style command), which would see stale-after-/new data. Note the gap is engine-agnostic — SessionController never reset context_usage on /new even on the v2 MetadataUpdated path; KAS-2b made it newly relevant because SessionController now tracks the KAS scalar too.

FIX (trivial, ~1 line each): add self.context_usage = None; to SessionController's SessionCreated and BridgeDisconnected arms, mirroring the UiState fix. No observable behavior change today.

PRECEDENT: cyril-85py tracks the analogous unread/divergent SessionController.steering_depth vs the optimistic UiState chip.

title: KAS terminals orphaned on Shutdown/bridge death — no kill_on_drop, no Shutdown-arm sweep
description: Found by the 2026-07-01 issue-set reliability review (grounded in code, not speculative): BridgeCommand::Shutdown aborts only prompt_task (bridge.rs:1363-1365); TerminalRegistry children are spawned WITHOUT kill_on_drop (terminal_io.rs:85-104), and when run_loop breaks, dropping the LocalSet kills nothing — live terminal children survive cyril exiting entirely. cyril-3lh8 covers only the session/cancel + turn-end reaping paths; this is the bridge-LIFETIME path (Shutdown, bridge thread death, app exit mid-command). Cheapest fix: .kill_on_drop(true) on the Command at create (also backstops the cyril-3lh8/cyril-r3t6 cancel-wedge interaction) + an explicit registry sweep in the Shutdown arm. Coordinate with cyril-3lh8's session-keyed reaping so the sweep reuses the same registry mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: No KAS-spawned terminal child outlives the cyril process: exiting cyril (or bridge Shutdown) while a long-running terminal command is in flight kills the child. Verified with a sleep-60 terminal command + a process-tree check after exit.

title: KAS-5b: drain terminal pipes at create (reader task) if KAS ever delays wait_for_exit
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) uses the 'wait does the draining' lifecycle: create spawns the child with piped stdout/stderr and returns the id; wait_for_exit moves the child out and calls child.wait_with_output().await, which drains both pipes concurrently with waiting. Safe because the 2.10.0 capture shows KAS calls wait_for_exit IMMEDIATELY after create (consecutive), so the undrained-pipe window is sub-millisecond. RISK: if a future KAS release delays wait_for_exit while the command is chatty (>64KB before wait arrives), the OS pipe buffer fills and the command blocks on write -> stall. Trigger to act: a capture showing KAS create-then-not-immediately-wait, OR a >64KB-output command stalling. Fix: drain-at-create reader task (spawn_local) that streams stdout+stderr into the entry buffer and reaps on exit (also enables true output-so-far + helps cyril-3lh8 reaping). Re-probe before assuming Option B is permanent.
notes: 2026-07-01 issue-set review — the gate is WRONG-BY-OMISSION: 'trigger to act: a future KAS release delaying wait' ignores that cyril-3lh8's cancel path already produces create-without-wait TODAY. A session/cancel landing between create and wait_for_exit means wait never comes; a command that has already written >64KB blocks forever on the full OS pipe (terminal_io.rs:85-104 pipes both streams with no reader until wait, :139) — a PERMANENT orphan plus a registry entry that never evicts. The two issues are mutually invalidating: 3lh8's 'orphan runs until it exits naturally' assumes drained pipes; this issue's 'sub-ms undrained window' assumes wait always arrives. Fix jointly with cyril-3lh8 (session-keyed registry + kill-on-cancel + drain-at-create reader); .kill_on_drop(true) at create is a cheap backstop either way. Keep P4 only if 3lh8's fix kills pre-wait children; otherwise this is effectively P3.

title: KAS-1 — Engine selection + _kiro/auth/getAccessToken responder (the entry gate)
description:
The precondition for every KAS milestone below. Without a host-supplied token, every KAS turn dies immediately ([TokenInvalidError] ... Host refresh callback returned no access token).

SCOPE (ROADMAP KAS-1, docs/ROADMAP.md:154):
- Make the KAS spawn real: the AgentEngine gate from KAS-0 (cyril-atjw) resolves the KAS engine to a live process; default stays v2. All new KAS code lands behind the 'kas' cargo feature from KAS-0.
  NOTE / CORRECTION: the engine flag was renamed 'kas' -> 'v3' as of kiro-cli 2.8.0 (probe-verified: 'kas' is rejected, possible values are v1/v2/v3; spawn is 'kiro-cli acp --agent-engine v3'). 2.7.1 accepted 'kas'. Resolve the engine string per installed version, don't hardcode 'kas'.
- Implement a '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' server->client request responder: reply {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. KAS validates expiresAt > now + ~3 min and REQUIRES profileArn (backend 400s 'profileArn is required' without it).
- Token sourcing — MIRROR Kiro's own responder, don't just read the sqlite row. Kiro (crates/chat-cli-v2/src/auth/{kas_token,refresh_coordinator,social,builder_id,external_idp}.rs): (1) resolves the active token across THREE types — social (GitHub -> kirocli:social:token, carries profile_arn), AWS Builder ID, external IdP (kirocli:external-idp:token); (2) proactively OIDC-refreshes before the ~3-min pre-expiry buffer via a lock-guarded refresh_coordinator; (3) answers {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn}. A naive 'read kirocli:social:token and return it' responder breaks on the refresh buffer (the failure our probes hit) and on Builder-ID/external-IdP users. Cleanest: DELEGATE to kiro-cli's own auth rather than reimplement OIDC refresh + multi-type resolution.
- This makes cyril a CUSTODIAN of a kiro credential — handle as such (no logging, read-only, minimal lifetime). See ROADMAP Open Tension #7.

This is the AUTH half of the initialize._meta.kiro (KiroClientMeta) handshake; the SETTINGS half is cyril-nhzw (AgentSettings).

REFS: ROADMAP KAS-1 (docs/ROADMAP.md:154); covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md (KiroClientMeta handshake); memory reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract.
acceptance_criteria:
* Engine-select plumbing resolves the KAS engine to a live spawn (version-correct flag: v3 on >=2.8.0, kas on 2.7.1); default remains v2.
* '_kiro/auth/getAccessToken' responder replies {accessToken, expiresAt, profileArn} and is exercised in a gated end-to-end smoke against a live KAS session.
* Auth-responder unit test covers the reply shape AND expired-token rejection (expiresAt <= now + ~3 min).
* profileArn is always present in the reply (Builder-ID / external-IdP users included), sourced by delegating to kiro-cli's own auth.
* Credential is never logged; token read is read-only and minimal-lifetime.
notes:
2026-06-21 — FREE PATH CONFIRMED; de-risks the entry gate (auth responder is no longer a hard prerequisite for a first live KAS turn).

Verified live: kiro-cli 2.8.1, @kiro/agent 0.3.257, experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-direct-spawn-2.8.1.py. KAS's selectAuthProvider resolves a --auth=user|machine|acp-callback flag (default user). The two spawn shapes differ:
  - WRAPPER spawn `kiro-cli acp --agent-engine v3` injects --auth=acp-callback -> KAS calls _kiro/auth/getAccessToken back on cyril -> the responder (this issue's main scope) is MANDATORY. This is the [TokenInvalidError] failure our earlier probes hit.
  - DIRECT spawn `node --experimental-wasm-modules <.../@kiro/agent/dist/server/acp-server.js> --transport=stdio` with NO --auth flag -> default FileAuthProvider reads ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json and self-refreshes via the file's refreshToken. _kiro/auth/getAccessToken fired 0 times across a full initialize + session/new + tool-using turn (stopReason end_turn). stderr logged "[INFO] Auth: default token file".

IMPLICATION: a first live KAS turn (cyril-j16p / KAS-2a) is reachable with ZERO credential-custodian code as long as the user has run `kiro-cli login`. Open Tension #7 moves OFF the walking-skeleton critical path.

SUGGESTED SPLIT (for triage, not yet applied):
  - tier-1 = free-path direct spawn, no responder. Unblocks the demo.
  - tier-2 = the _kiro/auth/getAccessToken acp-callback responder for the blessed wrapper lifecycle (kiro-cli token-sync + lock-guarded refresh-coordinator) and Builder-ID / external-IdP users.

Direct-spawn trade-off: cyril must locate the server entry (KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH env, else walk node_modules/@kiro/agent/dist/server/acp-server.js), the node runtime (KIRO_AGENT_PATH), and pass --experimental-wasm-modules — all of which the wrapper handles for free.

ROADMAP KAS-1 updated with this. Full --auth/--transport/sandbox flag contract + 5-tier auth priority in memory reference_kiro_kas_launch_contract.

2026-06-21 (fileMatch-steering probe session) — FOURTH token type for the tier-2 acp-callback responder, NOT covered by the three above: an IAM Identity Center / OIDC-PKCE login stores under auth_kv key `kirocli:odic:token` = {access_token, expires_at, refresh_token, region, start_url, oauth_flow, scopes} and carries NO profile_arn. For that case the matching profileArn lives in a SEPARATE row: auth_kv->`state` table, key `api.codewhisperer.profile`. Proven pitfall: pairing an OIDC accessToken with a DIFFERENT identity's profileArn (e.g. a stale ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token*.json, different account/region) -> backend 'Access denied'; accessToken and profileArn MUST be the same identity. Reinforces 'don't naively read one sqlite row': the responder must resolve profileArn per token type (social/Builder-ID embed it; odic splits it into state/api.codewhisperer.profile). Working assembly verified end-to-end in experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-filematch-steering-v2.py (odic access_token + state profileArn -> live KAS turn succeeded). Caveat for the direct-spawn 'free path': it reads ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json, but those files can be STALE/expired (they were expired this session for an odic-active user), so the free path depends on that file being kept fresh. See memory reference_kiro_kas_filematch_steering_source.

title: KAS context breakdown: per-file items[] drill-in (deferred from KAS-2b aggregate bar)
description: KAS context_usage frames carry items[] on the contextFiles + sessionFiles buckets (probe-verified 2.10.0, .cyril-5et2/context_usage_raw.json); tools/yourPrompts/kiroResponses are aggregate-only (no items). KAS-2b (cyril-5et2) renders only the aggregate category bar and deliberately does NOT model or render the per-file items[]. A follow-up can surface a per-file drill-in for contextFiles/sessionFiles under KAS, reusing the v2 /context append_context_items render helper. Out of scope for the KAS-2b bar; this is the tracker for the deferred drill-in.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — SoC CORRECTION, reword scope: do NOT reuse app.rs append_context_items (app.rs:1017-1052). It lives in the App binary and parses raw serde_json::Value from the v2 commands/execute response; reusing it for KAS either re-serializes typed data back to JSON or leaks raw KAS JSON past convert into App — both boundary regressions (CLAUDE.md: App must not parse JSON), and it couples a KAS feature to the v2 response-format dialect. Note ContextBucket deliberately omits an items field with a comment naming this issue — the no-drill-in invariant is type-enforced (types/session.rs:264-269).
Right shape: typed ContextItem (name/tokens/percent/flags) on ContextBucket in cyril-core, parsed in convert/kas.rs, rendered in cyril-ui. Reuse the presentation FORMAT, not the function. Bonus: creates the destination for eventually migrating the v2 /context formatting out of app.rs — shrinking a pre-existing App-layer violation instead of doubling down on it.

title: KAS-2a follow-up: turn_end vs prompt-response stop_reason fidelity on KAS cancel (first-to-arrive wins)
description:
Under KAS a turn produces TWO TurnCompleted: the agent's session_info_update -> turn_end (stop_reason from _meta.kiro.stopReason, scoped to the session) and the off-loop prompt-response synthesis (bridge.rs:563-574, sent as RoutedNotification::global). The bridge dedup (bridge.rs:1385) forwards whichever arrives FIRST and drops the second. For a normal turn both are EndTurn, so it is moot. On a USER CANCEL the prompt resolves Cancelled while turn_end's stopReason may differ; if turn_end wins the race carrying stopReason 'end_turn', the App records EndTurn instead of Cancelled — nondeterministic, dependent on KAS wire behavior on cancel (currently UNOBSERVED; no cancel turn_end has been captured).

Distinct from cyril-a71q: that residual is about clearing the WRONG turn; a per-turn id does NOT decide which of two same-turn signals carries the authoritative stop_reason. The design comment (bridge.rs:560-562) explicitly accepts 'either order' for the common (EndTurn) case.

ACTION before deciding a fix: capture a live KAS cancel (experiments/conductor-spike probe) — does turn_end fire on cancel? with what stopReason? If KAS already reports 'cancelled' in turn_end, there is no bug. If it reports 'end_turn' (or omits turn_end and only the prompt resolves Cancelled), decide whether the off-loop Cancelled must take precedence. Surfaced by /code-review max finders 2026-06-29. LOW severity: only manifests if KAS misreports the cancel stopReason in turn_end AND that frame wins the race.

title: KAS-0 — Engine trait + v2 port
description: Engine abstraction trait + port of current v2 (Rust) behavior behind it. Will later absorb K1a's three new convert/kiro.rs steering arms into the V2Engine impl at negligible cost. First milestone of the KAS engine integration track.
design:
KAS-0 design — synthesized via grill-with-docs (2026-06-21). Decisions D1-D9; full rationale in ADR-0004 + ADR-0001/0002. Acceptance = strict v2 behavioral parity (no visible change).

D1 Turn-end ownership: KAS-0 OWNS the v2 turn-end rewiring (not deferred to KAS-2a) — only way to prove the seam under v2 parity.
D2 Turn-end marker: no predicate/enum. Notification::TurnCompleted is the universal marker both engines converge on (v2 synthesizes from prompt() response; KAS maps session_info_update->turn_end). Busy-state becomes a flag cleared by observing the marker.
D3 Flag location: loop-local turn_in_flight: Option<SessionId>, set on SendPrompt, cleared when the loop observes TurnCompleted. Replaces prompt_task.is_finished() at bridge.rs:395 (busy-guard) + :455 (cancel-target). JoinHandle retained ONLY for Shutdown abort (bridge.rs:1233).
D4 Single-mediator loop (ADR-0004): run_loop select!s over an internal BridgeInbound{Notification,Request} channel. KiroClient + prompt_task feed it; loop forwards to App + clears flag on TurnCompleted. convert STAYS in KiroClient (loop forwards already-converted internal types). Engine shared as Rc<dyn Engine> (KiroClient=convert, loop=client_capabilities at bridge.rs:320).
D5 Request path through loop too, NON-BLOCKING (hard invariant): request_permission builds PermissionRequest with its embedded responder oneshot, sends to loop; loop forwards to App (identity mediation in v2); App replies on the oneshot BYPASSING the loop. Loop never awaits a human decision. Slow KAS-5 resolution spawns off-loop. Captured in ADR-0004.
D6 Capability sub-traits via defaulted accessors on Engine: fn as_auth_responder(&self)->Option<&dyn AuthResponder>{None}. KAS-0 stubs ONE worked example (AuthResponder, KAS-1's) + accessor; V2Engine overrides nothing. Open/closed: new surface = 1 sub-trait + 1 defaulted accessor, v2 untouched.
D7 AgentEngine{V2,Kas} typed selector (--agent-engine flag/config, default v2), NOT argv-sniffed from AgentCommand. run_bridge picks Box<dyn Engine> at spawn: V2=>V2Engine; Kas=>clean "not available yet" via existing error path. No KAS spawn / flag-version resolution (both KAS-1).
D8 kas cargo feature: cyril-core owns kas=[] (empty), cyril forwards kas=["cyril-core/kas"], cyril-ui none. Dedicated "kas-feature" CI job: build+clippy(-D warnings)+test -p cyril --features kas.
D9 Verification spike (parallel, NON-gating): reuse experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-* to capture a live KAS turn's session/update frames -> commit fixtures -> Rust test deserializes each into acp::SessionNotification, asserts Ok (CI-able, no live KAS). A failing variant = documented KAS-2a upgrade-trigger (no #[serde(other)] on SessionUpdate), not a KAS-0 blocker.

V2Engine = thin wrapper delegating to existing convert::session_update_to_notification (mod.rs:393) + convert::kiro::to_ext_notification (kiro.rs:173) — functions don't move.

Suggested slices (each ends green on all v2 tests): (1) Engine trait + V2Engine + client_capabilities-from-engine; (2) single-mediator loop (notifications + requests; flag replaces is_finished); (3) AgentEngine selector + run_bridge gate; (4) kas feature + AuthResponder stub + accessor + CI job; (5) verification spike fixtures+test.

Hazards handed forward — KAS-2a: post-turn_end, the still-running prompt task must not emit a competing late TurnCompleted, and a new SendPrompt must not orphan a live JoinHandle. KAS-5: slow request resolution spawns off-loop, never await in the loop (ADR-0004).
acceptance_criteria: Invisible foundation — ZERO user-visible change. Oracle is behavioral: (a) every existing v2 test passes; (b) a live `kiro-cli acp` session streams text/thoughts, runs tool calls, renders subagents, handles permission approve/cancel, and mode-picks IDENTICALLY to pre-refactor; (c) `--agent-engine kas` cleanly reports "not available yet" (no panic); (d) `kas-feature` CI job (build+clippy+test --features kas) is green; (e) verification-spike deser test passes on captured KAS session/update fixtures. Per-slice: each slice ends with all v2 tests + clippy -D warnings + fmt clean.
notes:
Reconstructed 2026-06-17 from .k1a-steering/related-issues.md; detail from ROADMAP KAS track.

Turn-end coupling (from PR #22 / cyril-84ca, 2026-06-17): the off-loop prompt dispatch made TurnCompleted be emitted when conn.prompt().await resolves, and the one-turn busy-guard keys off prompt_task.is_finished(). Both assume 'prompt response == turn end' — true for v2, FALSE for KAS (KAS-2a: turn-end is session_info_update->turn_end; the prompt response is secondary/late). Under KAS, is_finished() stays false after a logical turn-end, so a legitimate next prompt is wrongly rejected with 'a turn is already in progress', and TurnCompleted fires late/never. When this milestone introduces the Engine trait's turn-end detection and ports v2 behind V2Engine, route BOTH the busy-guard and TurnCompleted emission through the engine's turn-end signal, NOT prompt_task.is_finished()/prompt().await. Concrete code: bridge.rs SendPrompt guard + the spawned prompt_task.

title: KAS-2b — Live context-usage breakdown bar (session_info_update:context_usage)
description:
On KAS (v3), the per-category context breakdown is PUSHED proactively on the wire — so cyril can render a live categorized context bar (Files / Tools / Prompts / Responses) in the toolbar WITHOUT the user running /context. On v1/v2 the breakdown only exists as the pulled /context command response; the streaming _kiro.dev/metadata notification there carries only the scalar contextUsagePercentage.

WIRE SHAPE (probe-verified, kiro-cli 2.8.0, 2026-06-19, live KAS turn):
- session/update -> session_info_update, _meta.kiro.kind == 'context_usage', payload {usagePercentage, breakdown?} (covenant ContextUsageBreakdown).
- breakdown = 5 buckets {contextFiles, tools, kiroResponses, yourPrompts, sessionFiles}, each {tokens, percent, items?}.
- ~6 frames pushed per turn.

CONSTRAINTS THAT SHAPE THE UI (all probe-verified):
1. tools bucket is AGGREGATE-ONLY — items is structurally absent (live: {tokens:33703, percent:3.4}). Only contextFiles/sessionFiles ever carry items[]. Do NOT build per-tool drill-down; the wire cannot support it on either engine.
2. breakdown is OPTIONAL PER FRAME — some context_usage frames carry usagePercentage only (breakdown absent). Retain last non-absent; treat absence as 'no update', not 'cleared' (same discipline as the effort field).
3. cyril's existing /context path (kiro.dev/commands/execute) is REJECTED by KAS (-32603 'no persistence classification'). Must read the streaming notification, not the command surface.

MAPPING: breakdown is richer than cyril's flat ContextUsage/TokenCounts — either map usagePercentage to ContextUsage + extend, or model the breakdown as a new type. The itemized buckets can reuse the existing render helper append_context_items (crates/cyril/src/app.rs:900), today wired only to the pulled /context path.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-2a (walking skeleton — turn renders+ends via session_info_update:turn_end; not yet filed). Part of the KAS-2 _kiro/* dialect track, anchored on KAS-0 (cyril-atjw).

REFS: probe experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-context-breakdown-2.8.0.py; ROADMAP KAS-2b (docs/ROADMAP.md:179).
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril renders a live categorized context bar fed by session_info_update:context_usage frames — no /context command issued.
* Per-frame 'breakdown absent' is handled: last breakdown retained, not cleared.
* tools bucket renders as an aggregate row (no per-tool UI); contextFiles/sessionFiles render itemized when items[] is present.
* v1/v2 engine behavior is unchanged (still uses the pulled /context command response).
* State + render tests cover (a) a breakdown-absent frame and (b) the aggregate-only tools bucket.
notes:
2026-06-21 — Re-confirmed the 5-bucket breakdown independently (experiments/conductor-spike/probe-kas-context-shows-steering.py): contextFiles itemized the file the agent read; tools aggregate-only (33703 tokens, no items); the v2 /context (kiro.dev/commands/execute) rejected. RELEVANT NEGATIVE for this bar: the breakdown has NO steering bucket and does not itemize steering docs. Steering is a SEPARATE channel (_kiro/steering/documents_changed catalog + session_info_update:steering_inclusion) — so do NOT try to source an 'active rules/steering' view from the context bar; it isn't there. See memory reference_kiro_kas_filematch_steering_source.

Closed: Implemented and merged via PR #33 (merge 9aca2fe); follow-up review fixes in 56f57ec. Deferred items tracked: cyril-1116 (per-file drill-in), cyril-mdbp (narrow-terminal overflow), cyril-cx27 (SessionController lifecycle divergence).

title: KAS-8 — MCP management subsystem under KAS (_kiro/mcp/*)
description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/mcp/*` — the MCP-management subsystem under KAS. v2 exposes MCP via the `/mcp` slash command; KAS exposes it as this `_kiro/*` dialect, which cyril has no handling for.

Surface (covenant):
- Notifications (agent->client): `_kiro/mcp/status`, `_kiro/mcp/governance_disabled`.
- Requests (client->agent): `_kiro/mcp/resetServer {serverName, startOAuth?}`, `_kiro/mcp/getPrompt {serverName, promptName, arguments?}`, `_kiro/mcp/getResource {serverName, uri}`.
- Host callback (agent->client request, gated by advertising): `_kiro/mcp/elicitation {sessionId, toolCallId, elicitation}` -> ElicitationResolveResponse.

SCOPE:
- Consume `_kiro/mcp/status` / `governance_disabled` for an MCP panel (server list + state), the KAS analog of v2 `/mcp`.
- Wire `resetServer` (incl. the OAuth-reset flow — relevant to the 2.8.1 MCP-OAuth clipboard fix) and optionally getPrompt/getResource.
- Decide whether to advertise `mcp/elicitation` (pairs with the userInput / richer-question overlay from CN1).

CAVEAT: this user runs 0 MCP servers, so live capture needs an MCP server configured first.

REFS: covenant §1b/§1c (mcp methods); ROADMAP KAS-8 coverage matrix + KAS-4 (/code,/usage panels are the sibling pattern); memory reference_kiro_2_8_1_diff (MCP OAuth clipboard fix).

title: Bridge command loop blocks on conn.prompt() for the whole turn — no mid-turn commands (steer/cancel)
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) proved: bridge.rs awaits conn.prompt().await INSIDE the single-consumer command loop (while let Some(cmd)=command_rx.recv()), so it never recv()s a queued command until the turn ends. Wire oracle: SteerSession enqueued at +1.5s did not cross the wire until 12.839s, 1ms after the session/prompt response at 12.838s. Consequences: (1) mid-turn steering (K1b core) is impossible as-is; (2) STRONGLY IMPLICATED but not separately probed: Esc-while-busy CancelRequest goes through the same loop, so mid-turn cancel is also non-functional today. Fix direction: drive the prompt off the command loop (spawn_local the conn.prompt() future; resolve TurnCompleted from that task) so the loop stays free to process SteerSession/CancelRequest concurrently. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A command enqueued mid-turn is sent to the agent before TurnCompleted (probe: steer surfaces before turn end). Esc-cancel aborts a busy turn promptly. Regression fence: an integration test driving the bridge with a slow turn + a mid-turn command, asserting the command's effect precedes turn completion.
notes: Implementation complete on fix/bridge-loop-midturn-commands (7 slices, checkpointed-build). Off-loop prompt dispatch: SendPrompt spawn_local's the turn on an Rc<conn>; one-in-flight guard via JoinHandle::is_finished; Shutdown aborts. Fences C1(loop frees) C2(steer mid-turn) C3(cancel resolves Cancelled) C4(single turn) C6(one TurnCompleted) C7(shutdown) C9(guard self-clears) all pass via an in-process fake-agent harness; 734 workspace tests + clippy + fmt clean; prove-it oracles (test_bridge, steer/cancel/notif_order probes) agree vs real kiro 2.8.0. C5 (ordering) resolved empirically — holds for v2 AND KAS, not a cyril-84ca property; theoretical zero-gap library race filed cyril-9akh. Ready for PR; unblocks K1b (cyril-bm1j).

title: KAS context bar can overflow the status line on narrow terminals (clips credits + scroll hint)
description:
Introduced by KAS-2b (cyril-5et2, PR #33). render_status_bar (crates/cyril-ui/src/widgets/toolbar.rs:136) appends the 5-label KAS context breakdown bar ('Context Files X%  Session Files Y%  Tools Z%  Prompts P%  Responses R%', ~70 cols) to the same single, non-wrapping Line that already carries 'Context: N%', credits, tokens, stop-reason, and the browse-mode 'SCROLL ↓ PgDn' hint. The Paragraph renders without wrap, so on a narrow terminal (e.g. 80 cols) everything past the right edge is clipped.

REPRO: run a KAS session in an ~80-col terminal; once a context_usage frame with a breakdown arrives, both 'Context: N%' and the 5-label bar render. The breakdown bar is pushed BEFORE credits and the scroll hint, so those later segments fall off-screen — hiding the affordance that tells the user how to leave browse/follow mode.

Pre-existing single-line truncation mechanism, but the wide unconditional bar newly makes it reachable. Graceful (no panic) — purely a layout/UX regression.

FIX is a design choice, not a one-liner: e.g. a compact/responsive form when width is constrained, eliding the redundant scalar when the breakdown is present, abbreviating labels, or measuring remaining width before appending. Out of scope for the KAS-2b bar itself; surfaced by /code-review max on PR #33.

REFS: toolbar.rs:115-156 (render_status_bar); related cyril-5et2 (KAS-2b).

title: KAS — Marshal kiro-cli settings into the _meta.kiro.settings (AgentSettings) handshake
description:
On KAS (v3), per-session feature flags ride the wire as AgentSettings in the initialize handshake: clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings (KiroClientMeta; covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3, lines 98-104). cyril sends only {cwd, mcpServers} with NO _meta.kiro.settings, so on KAS EVERY feature flag is off: toolSearch (MCP token savings), thinking, knowledge, codeIntelligence, subagentOrchestration, compaction, todoList, checkpoint, semanticReview, fta, tangentMode, disableAutoCompaction, and the _c2s/_subagent/_delegate/_providerPowers/_parallelTasks/etc. family. KAS thus runs in a degraded all-flags-off mode.

The v2 TUI assembles this object from the kiro-cli settings file (function zme() in tui.js) and hands it to the ACP client constructor. cyril has no equivalent and no AgentSettings type (called out as a gap in covenant section 10).

CONTRAST WITH v2 (why this is KAS-only): the v2 Rust backend reads toolSearch (and other settings) DIRECTLY from the kiro-cli settings file across the ACP boundary — free for cyril, same pattern as trust-persistence. KAS is a separate Node process that cannot read the Rust settings store, so the client must marshal settings over the wire. See memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting.

SCOPE: read kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and build _meta.kiro.settings matching the AgentSettings shape, sent in the KAS initialize handshake. Default-sensitive flags (covenant section 3): semanticReview default ON if absent; fta default OFF; subagentOrchestration true -> orchestrate_subagent tool, false/absent -> invoke_sub_agent (CHANGES the subagent tool — directly affects KAS-3 rendering); toolSearch: {enabled, minPct, minTokens, neverDefer}; knowledge/compaction nested objects.

WHY IT MATTERS: gates MCP token savings via toolSearch (governs the tools-bucket size in cyril-5et2's context bar), thinking, and subagent-engine selection (KAS-3). Foundational enabler shared by multiple downstream KAS milestones.

DEPENDS ON: KAS-1 auth responder (KAS sessions must actually run first; not yet filed as its own issue) and the KAS-0 engine gate (cyril-atjw). This is the SETTINGS half of the same initialize._meta.kiro handshake whose AUTH half is KAS-1.

ROADMAP NOTE: the ROADMAP currently spells out only the auth half of the KAS-1 handshake; AgentSettings appears only piecemeal (subagentOrchestration at docs/ROADMAP.md:209). Recommend making AgentSettings explicit under KAS-1 (doc edit, separate from this ticket).

REFS: covenant docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md section 3; memory reference_kiro_tool_search_setting; v2 TUI zme().
acceptance_criteria:
* On the KAS engine, cyril sends clientCapabilities._meta.kiro.settings in initialize, populated from kiro-cli settings (global + workspace) and matching the AgentSettings shape.
* toolSearch.{enabled,minPct,minTokens} is forwarded and observably takes effect on KAS (tool_search advertised, MCP tools deferred out of the loaded set).
* Default-sensitive flags handled correctly: semanticReview default-ON-if-absent, fta default-OFF, subagentOrchestration selects orchestrate_subagent vs invoke_sub_agent.
* A new AgentSettings type in cyril-core with serde camelCase, unit-tested for wire shape + the default-sensitive flags.
* v1/v2 engine path is unchanged (no _meta.kiro.settings sent or required there).

title: K1a — Queue-steering wire + state plumbing
description: Wire + state machinery for Kiro 2.7.0 queue steering. Converter arm for _kiro.dev/session/update (steering_queued/consumed/cleared -> notifications), bridge SteerSession/ClearSteering as awaited _session/steer[/clear] ExtRequests with a -32601 emit-once unsupported gate, and SessionController/UiState mirrors (steering_depth, steering_unsupported, steering_queued). No UX — that is K1b.
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17 after the working-tree rivets DB was lost (committed empty on the chore branch). Done: merged to main via PR #18 (a8e1037) + #19 (253ff46) review follow-ups.

title: KAS terminal registry: kill/release during pending wait_for_exit are silent no-ops; release-then-wait resurrects the freed id
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review (perf/reliability lens) — untracked by any existing terminal issue (distinct from cyril-3lh8 orphan-on-cancel, cyril-ba5x Shutdown orphans, cyril-r3t6 pipe drain).

wait() takes the Child out of the registry (Entry::Running(None)) and awaits wait_with_output(). The acp rpc layer spawns every inbound request as its own concurrent spawn_local task (verified in agent-client-protocol-0.10.2 rpc.rs handle_incoming), so terminal/kill or terminal/release arriving WHILE wait is pending hits take_child -> Taken::InFlight, and both handlers use `if let Taken::Child(..)` — the InFlight case FALLS THROUGH and returns Ok without signaling the process:

- kill (terminal_io.rs:213): returns KillTerminalResponse having killed nothing. The module's own docs say KAS calls wait immediately after create (:16-17) and that kill-then-read-partial-output is KAS's command-timeout pattern (:205-208) — so EVERY kill arrives during a pending wait. Failure: KAS runs a hung command via create+wait; on its timeout it sends kill -> cyril replies Ok, process keeps running; the pending wait never resolves; the turn hangs (or the hung child leaks as a live orphan forever).
- release (terminal_io.rs:188): same fall-through (child not killed), then :200 unconditionally remove()s the id. When the still-pending wait() later completes, :151 insert()s an Exited entry BACK under the released id — violating the released-id -> -32602 contract and leaking the entry + captured output for the life of the bridge.

Root cause is one spot (the InFlight fall-through). A fix needs a kill signal that does not require owning the Child — e.g. retain the pid at create, or store a kill handle before wait takes the Child. The single-request-at-a-time tests never exercise the concurrent case.
acceptance_criteria: kill during a pending wait actually terminates the process and the pending wait resolves with the killed status; release during a pending wait kills the child and the id stays unknown (-32602) after the wait completes (no re-insert); concurrency tests added for kill-during-wait and release-during-wait on a current_thread runtime.

title: KAS layer idiomatic cleanup batch (2026-07-01 implementation review)
description:
Small idiomatic/silent-failure items from the 2026-07-01 implementation review, batched. Each is independently landable; none changes behavior on the happy path.

1. thiserror migration: version.rs returns Result<_, String> (:10,34,47,72); discovery.rs KasMissing has structured variants but implements neither Display nor Error (hand-rolled reason(), flattened at bridge.rs:303 via .map_err(|m| m.reason())); auth.rs internal plumbing is Result<_, String> (:51,137). CLAUDE.md mandates thiserror with structured metadata; string errors cannot be pattern-matched — this bites when cyril-taba must distinguish stale/missing/corrupt token programmatically. KasMissing is 90% there: #[error(...)] attributes would delete reason() outright.
2. auth.rs:152-166 — log (debug!/warn!) before returning Err to KAS on every error path (missing home, unreadable token, missing profileArn, stale). Sibling modules log per the surface-do-not-swallow rule (host_io.rs:94, terminal_io.rs:304-320); auth failures present as opaque dead turns with nothing in cyril.log.
3. convert/kas.rs:43-45 — log before returning None when a context_usage frame lacks usagePercentage; the same function already warns for present-but-unparseable breakdown (:56). Wire drift here would silently freeze the context % display.
4. discovery.rs:111 — NoHome reason is misleading when KIRO_KAS_SERVER_PATH is set (the bundle WAS located via the override; the reason says to set the var that is already set). Add token-path context to that arm; the test matrix (:266) covers no-home only without overrides.
5. client.rs:280 — debug!-log the method name of unhandled _kiro/* ext requests before returning the null default; @kiro/agent adds host callbacks nearly every release and a new required callback currently degrades invisibly.
6. Named constants for the JSON-RPC codes -32602/-32603/-32000 (raw literals in ~12 places across auth.rs, host_io.rs, terminal_io.rs, client.rs).
7. settings.rs:103-172 — rename the minified-JS identifiers (e, n, c, k, ts) carried over from the zme() transcription; replicate semantics, not names.
8. terminal_io.rs:287 — one-line comment on the `code as u32` sign-loss cast (Windows negative NTSTATUS wraps to the conventional unsigned form — the reasoning is sound but appears nowhere).
9. version.rs:19-22 — drop the dead .unwrap_or("") on the infallible first split().next(); the .filter(!is_empty) does the real work.
10. bridge.rs:825-838 — QueryCommandOptions RPC failure collapses to CommandOptionsReceived { options: [] }, indistinguishable from a command with genuinely no options (a PARSE error correctly emits BridgeError). Emit BridgeError for the RPC-failure case too.
11. client.rs:130-140 (pre-existing) — on ext-notification params parse failure, early-return after the error! instead of substituting Value::Null and double-logging through the engine path.
12. settings.rs:63-66 — debug! on the no-home empty-settings fallback (the module's only unlogged fallback; its own doc warns this is exactly the bare-fallback-flags failure shape). Also fix the doc: initialize is once per BRIDGE, not once per session.
13. Document (or make async) the two remaining sync call sites — settings.rs:26 std::fs via KasEngine::client_capabilities (engine.rs:107) and version.rs:48-51 std::process — both provably startup-only, but the layer's own invariant (host_io.rs:15-17) says never call sync fs/process on this runtime, and neither site documents why blocking is acceptable there.

title: KAS auth: --auth=machine / KIRO_API_KEY bypass _kiro/auth/getAccessToken — simpler fallback path
description: Static analysis of @kiro/agent (present in 0.3.299 AND 0.8.0, i.e. not new but previously undocumented for us): acp-server.js selectAuthProvider priority = (1) KIRO_API_KEY env -> EnvAuthProvider, (2) --auth=acp-callback -> AcpCallbackAuthProvider (current cyril path, host-mediated refresh, profileArn gotcha), (3) --auth=machine -> FileAuthProvider plain-text token at tokenPath or /tmp/access_token, (4) explicit tokenPath JSON, (5) default ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json. Modes 1/3/4/5 never call _kiro/auth/getAccessToken. Option value: degraded-mode fallback when the callback path breaks (e.g. profileArn rotation bugs), and a simpler harness for probes. Note kiro-cli's own spawn of KAS may pin --auth=acp-callback; direct-spawn is where this pays off.
acceptance_criteria: Documented in KAS integration notes; optionally a bridge config knob to direct-spawn KAS with --auth=machine for diagnostics
notes: Closed: Already covered: launch-contract memory (2026-06-21 server-side ground truth) documents selectAuthProvider priority incl. --auth=machine/KIRO_API_KEY, and the no-callback free path was empirically confirmed on 2.8.1; cyril-taba tracks the practical token-staleness gap. 2.11.0 audit confirmed the auth layer is byte-identical in 0.8.0 — no new work item.

title: Multi-client observer: count/echo steers cyril didn't originate (wire steering_queued)
description:
When cyril-7z7u makes the steer chip OPTIMISTIC (incremented at add_steer_echo / user-send) and stops incrementing on the wire SteeringQueued notification (to avoid double-counting cyril's own steers), a steer that ANOTHER client on the same session originates — surfaced only as a wire SteeringQueued with no local optimistic echo — will not be counted or echoed by cyril.

This is fine for cyril's single-client model TODAY. It becomes a gap if/when the multi-client observer direction lands (ROADMAP session-level workflows / _kiro/sessions/changed). At that point, reintroduce wire-SteeringQueued handling for steers WITHOUT a matching local echo (observer-originated), without double-counting cyril's own optimistic steers — likely needs a correlation key the wire doesn't currently provide (content is advisory).

REF: cyril-7z7u falsifiable-design (.cyril-7z7u/falsifiable-design.md), negative space.

title: Streamed agent text can render after TurnCompleted (ACP notification-vs-response ordering race)
description: agent-client-protocol (rpc.rs) resolves incoming RESPONSES inline in the read loop (oneshot send) but routes incoming NOTIFICATIONS through a separate incoming_tx queue handled by another task. So when a session/update notification immediately precedes the prompt response on the wire (zero scheduling gap), the response (-> TurnCompleted) can overtake the not-yet-handled notification on cyril's notification channel, committing TurnCompleted before the final streamed AgentMessage. Discovered during cyril-84ca Slice 7 via a zero-gap unit harness. NOT introduced by cyril-84ca (the pre-fix inline conn.prompt().await shares the identical race). Empirically does NOT manifest against real kiro 2.8.0 v2 OR KAS (.cyril-84ca/notif_order.rs: order is [msg,msg,TURN] for v2, [msg,TURN] for KAS) because real backends leave enough timing gap for the notification queue to drain. Low priority: theoretical/edge race, no observed production impact. Possible fixes if it ever surfaces: have the turn task yield/drain pending notifications before emitting TurnCompleted, or coalesce turn-end behind the notification queue.
acceptance_criteria: A unit/integration test reproduces the reorder deterministically AND a fix makes streamed AgentMessages always precede TurnCompleted on the notification channel even at zero wire-gap; verified to still hold against real v2 + KAS.
notes:
2026-06-21 — Real-world corroboration: upstream Kiro bug #9542 (https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/9542) shows the parent agent going Idle while a result is still in flight (trace WARN: 'received a tool execution event for an agent not processing tools ... active_state: Idle, executing_subagents: {}'), dropping the subagent's late result — the same parent-races-its-own-late-result shape as this race, observed in production. Caveat: that bug is the parent<->subagent channel INSIDE kiro (upstream of cyril's ACP wire), so it doesn't directly hit cyril's notification channel, but it proves late/dropped results around turn-end are not purely theoretical. Better repro tool than the synthetic zero-gap harness: KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH=/tmp/acp-trace.jsonl captures full ACP wire traffic as {ts,dir,msg} JSONL (tui.js, confirmed 2.8.1) — use it to capture real notification-vs-response ordering at near-zero gap. Verify any fix on BOTH v2 and v3/KAS (upstream failure mode is engine-specific). Subagent-rendering hazards from #9542 tracked in cyril-fjfu (KAS-3).

2026-07-02 — Ordering ground-truth from a real KAS session (KIRO_ACP_RECORD_PATH capture, exactly the tool this note recommended). Redacted snapshot committed at experiments/conductor-spike/kas-live-session-trace-2.11.0.jsonl (527 frames/880s, 2 turns: id=5 cancelled, id=8 completed). On BOTH completed turn boundaries the ONLY session/update trailing turn_end within 5s is a single session_info_update::context_usage (~10ms after the prompt response); NO agent_message_chunk or tool_call arrives after turn_end on either turn. So in this KAS/v3 session the streamed-text-after-TurnCompleted race did NOT manifest on the wire — only benign context telemetry trails turn_end (matches the 'context_usage trails turn_end' note in kas.rs). CAVEAT: v3/KAS only, 2 turns (one cancelled) = one sample, not proof; still verify any fix on BOTH v2 and v3. This is evidence the race is timing-window-narrow at the source, consistent with it being a cyril-side reordering risk rather than a routine backend emission.

title: K1c — Queue-steering polish + parity (optional)
description: Optional; evaluate after K1b. Queue-mode parity with Kiro's Ctrl+S toggle: a client-side buffer that flushes to session/prompt on TurnCompleted instead of steering mid-turn (pure cyril-side, no new wire). Subagent steering: probe _session/steer against subagent session ids, then wire /steer @<name> <msg> through SubagentTracker::find_by_name if it works.
notes: Reconstructed 2026-06-17.

title: KAS host-io loop-mediation seam (ADR-0004 gate/transform point for fs+terminal)
description:
Deferred from KAS-5a (cyril-7bdu) during checkpointed-build. ADR-0004 decided that server->client host-io requests (fs, terminal) route THROUGH the bridge run_loop's host-io arm so the engine's capability sub-traits / a cyril-stages gate can observe/transform them. KAS-5a instead resolves fs DIRECTLY in #[cfg(feature=kas)] KiroClient overrides (kas::host_io::{read,write}_text_file) because: (1) the loop seam has no consumer yet — no stages gate exists (the codebase's engine.rs:12 rule says capability sub-traits land WITH their first consumer, not as consumer-less stubs); (2) routing through run_loop forces a #[cfg]'d run_loop parameter (can't cleanly cfg a single fn param) — either ship HostIoRequest+channel un-gated in every v2 build (violates ADR-0002 'default build links no KAS code') or maintain two cfg'd run_loop signatures; (3) the non-blocking invariant (KAS-5a claim C4) is already satisfied WITHOUT the loop arm — the acp ClientSideConnection spawns each inbound request as its own spawn_local task (rpc.rs:272, wired bridge.rs:390), so a direct async tokio::fs resolve is off-loop and never starves the loop.

WHEN TO BUILD THIS: when the first real consumer of the seam lands — a cyril-stages host-io gate (audit/policy/transform of file reads+writes+shell execs, ADR-0003/Phase 2) or KAS-7 hooks. At that point, move fs+terminal resolution behind a HostIoRequest channel + run_loop arm so the consumer can intercept, and resolve ADR-0002's cfg-threading (likely: gate the whole host-io module + a kas-only run_loop variant, or accept un-gated host-io once it's no longer kas-only). Until then, direct-resolve in the cfg'd overrides is the right altitude.

REFS: ADR-0004 (docs/adr/0004); .cyril-7bdu/falsifiable-design.md claim C3 + Negative-space #3; KAS-5a cyril-7bdu.

title: KAS-5b — terminal host-callback responders (terminal/* + shell_type)
description:
KAS-5b: the terminal half of host I/O, split from cyril-7bdu (KAS-5a = fs read/write). Implement the typed acp::Client terminal methods cyril must answer when it advertises the terminal capability: create_terminal {sessionId, command, args, cwd}, terminal_output {sessionId, terminalId}, wait_for_terminal_exit {sessionId, terminalId}, release_terminal, kill_terminal (+ _kiro/terminal/shell_type {sessionId}). Wire shapes captured @ 2.10.0 in .cyril-7bdu/host_callbacks_2.10.0.json (bare ACP methods, every call carries sessionId, cwd absolute).
