REUSES the KAS-5a seam (.cyril-7bdu/falsifiable-design.md): cap advertisement via Engine::client_capabilities() (add terminal:true), the ADR-0004 loop host-io mediation arm, the non-blocking-resolver invariant, and platform::path translation. Terminal STRESSES the non-blocking invariant hardest: a shell command can run up to 60s, so create_terminal must return a terminalId immediately and wait_for_terminal_exit must await via tokio::process (NOT a thread-pinning std::process wait) or it starves the single-threaded bridge runtime (rpc.rs spawns each request, but the runtime is current_thread+LocalSet). terminalIds must be unique across concurrent terminals; output/wait/release/kill on an unknown id must error, not panic. create with a nonexistent command must error, not panic the bridge.

DEPENDS ON KAS-5a (cyril-7bdu) for the seam. Run its own falsifiable-design pass before build.
notes: Closed: Merged in PR #36 (feat/kas-5-host-io -> main, c89a1f0). fs (5a) + terminal (5b) host-callback responders shipped; review fixes (stdin=null, logged reaps, wedge-on-error, cross-platform test gating) in a128633. CI green on Linux/macOS/Windows. Follow-ups remain as separate issues (3lh8, 0v42, 8tq6, g9vt, r3t6, 1rpv, ihj1).

title: KAS-5b follow-up: reap/kill a session's live terminals on cancel/turn-end (orphan-on-cancel)
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) spawns child processes on the bridge for terminal/create and reaps them on terminal/{release,kill} (the normal KAS flow: create->wait_for_exit->output->release). But if a turn is CANCELLED mid-command, KAS may not send release -> the child keeps running until it exits naturally (e.g. a 60s sleep = a 60s orphan on the bridge process). Scope: on session/cancel (and/or turn-end) kill that session's still-running terminals, keyed by session_id in the terminal registry. Needs the registry to track session_id per terminal (it carries sessionId on every request). Decide policy: kill-on-cancel vs let-finish. Out of KAS-5b's first cut (the happy-path lifecycle); this is the leak-on-cancel edge.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) TRIGGER SITE unnamed in the issue — mechanism (TerminalRegistry, owned by KiroClient at client.rs:26) and trigger (run_loop's Cancel arm bridge.rs:582-598 + TurnCompleted observation) sit on opposite sides of exactly the seam cyril-g9vt deferred. Decide JOINTLY with cyril-g9vt whether this is the ADR-0004 seam's first consumer. Minimal, proportionate option for a P3 leak fix: share Rc<TerminalRegistry> between KiroClient and the loop (same LocalSet thread; registry stays sole owner of process lifecycle) and call reap_session(session_id) from the Cancel arm. A third ad-hoc wiring (e.g. App sending a new reap BridgeCommand) would erode both this and g9vt.
(2) 'Keyed by session_id' should mean a session_id FIELD on Entry (from CreateTerminalRequest.session_id) + linear-scan reap_session — terminal id stays the primary key; a secondary index is over-engineering for a handful of terminals. Reap must follow the existing take-then-await pattern (no RefCell borrow across .await, terminal_io.rs:238-249).
(3) Policy recommendation: apply the existing KILL semantics (id stays valid, partial output cached), NOT release — KAS may still send terminal/output/release for those ids after the cancel, and kill-semantics lets them resolve instead of erroring -32602. Cancel-only for the first cut; reap-at-turn-end can race an in-flight release.
(4) INTERACTION with cyril-r3t6 (see its note): a cancelled turn whose command already wrote >64KB is wedged on the full OS pipe and never exits naturally — kill-on-cancel must also cover pre-wait children, and .kill_on_drop(true) at create is a cheap backstop.

title: KAS host-io: atomic write_text_file (temp + rename) to avoid corrupting files on partial write
description:
kas::host_io::write_text_file does tokio::fs::write (truncate + write) directly at the target path. A crash, disk-full, or error mid-write leaves a truncated/corrupt file in the user's working tree with the original content gone.

Since cyril is now the executor of the agent's writes (ADR-0003), durable writes belong at this layer: write to a temp file in the SAME directory, fsync, then atomic rename over the target -- matching the project's session-sidecar MOVED_TO cadence and the rust-best-practices atomic-write guidance. Decide behavior for symlink targets and permission/ownership preservation. Keep the existing mkdir -p (create_dir_all) for missing parents. Discovered during review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: An interrupted write never leaves a partial file at the target (original preserved, or fully-new content -- never half); temp + rename happens within the target's directory; empty-content and parent-dir-creation tests still pass; symlink-target behavior documented.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review — PROMOTED P3->P2: since ADR-0003 makes cyril the executor of every KAS agent write, this is the only open issue whose failure mode is silent user-data loss (bare tokio::fs::write truncate-in-place, host_io.rs:52). Land before KAS becomes a daily driver.

Two footguns the plan must solve concretely (not just 'decide behavior'):
(1) Permission clobbering — NamedTempFile creates mode 0600 and persist() silently downgrades an existing 0644/0755 target. fs::metadata(target) + set_permissions on the temp BEFORE rename; umask-respecting mode for fresh files.
(2) Symlink write-through — rename replaces a symlink with a regular file (today's tokio::fs::write writes THROUGH it). canonicalize before choosing temp dir + rename target.

Implementation shape: whole sequence in ONE tokio::task::spawn_blocking with sync tempfile::NamedTempFile::new_in(parent) + write_all + sync_all + persist — single threadpool hop, temp auto-cleans on failure via Drop. Update the host_io.rs:9-18 doc comment (spawn_blocking is the sanctioned sync form). Dir-fsync optional: its failure mode is 'old content survives', which the AC permits; if added, unix-gate it (dir sync_all fails on Windows). Housekeeping: tempfile is currently dev-only in cyril-core (Cargo.toml) — promote via { workspace = true }.

title: acp rpc layer buffers notifications unboundedly under UI stall (no agent-side backpressure)
description: Tracking note from the 2026-07-01 issue-set reliability review — filed so the failure signature is searchable; no action until observed. The vendored agent-client-protocol read loop pushes every notification through incoming_tx.unbounded_send (rpc.rs handle_io) and spawns each handler as its own local task, which then awaits cyril's BOUNDED inbound channel (cap 256, bridge.rs:16/374). Verified during review: no deadlock is possible at the rpc layer (responses resolve inline; the 32-slot command channel circular wait is not realistically reachable) — but no backpressure ever reaches the agent process either. If the App stalls (worst realistic case: tty flow-control-stopped via Ctrl+S while terminal.draw blocks, app.rs:228+) during a heavy KAS streaming turn, the unbounded queue plus parked handler tasks grow without bound for the duration of the turn. Memory growth bounded by turn size, not a deadlock. Related: cyril-9akh lives in the same rpc.rs notification-vs-response plumbing.

title: Optimistic steer chip can stick >0 when a turn-tail steer never drains (no next turn / non-EndTurn / backend drop)
description: cyril-7z7u removed the unconditional TurnCompleted reset of UiState.steering_queued so a steer DEFERRED by the backend to the next turn keeps its chip across turn-end (correct per probe). Residual: the optimistic chip now drains ONLY via SteeringConsumed/Cleared/Unsupported/SessionCreated. If a steer is queued at a turn's tail and NO draining event ever arrives, the chip shows a phantom count until /new. Reachable paths: (a) user steers as the turn ends, then sends no further prompt (no next turn -> no deferred SteeringConsumed); (b) turn ends non-EndTurn / cancelled / errored with the steer un-consumed; (c) backend accepts the steer (Ok queued:true, bridge emits nothing) but never streams a consume. The old code masked all of these by resetting every turn-end. Decide a BOUNDED safety net that does not regress the deferred-steer case (e.g. reset on Activity::Idle rather than Ready, or on the 2nd consecutive TurnCompleted with no intervening SteeringConsumed) — needs the .cyril-7z7u probe to confirm whether consume is guaranteed-eventual. Found by /code-review of PR 28 (finding #1). state.rs:418 TurnCompleted arm.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) The Activity::Idle option is a near-no-op — Idle is set only at startup and on BridgeDisconnected (state.rs:475 / app.rs:166); TurnCompleted sets Ready (state.rs:462), so an Idle-keyed reset never drains the chip during a live session. Rule it out.
(2) Prefer the 2-consecutive-TurnCompleted-without-SteeringConsumed rule: heuristic but CONTRACT-BACKED by the .cyril-7z7u probe (a tail steer's queued+consumed pair defers to exactly the next turn, so two turn-ends without a consume means the steer is provably gone) — still gated on the probe confirming consume-is-guaranteed-eventual, as the issue says.
(3) Path (a) is TRUTHFUL, not phantom: per the probe the steer really is still queued backend-side through an idle tail and will consume when a next turn starts. A chip persisting through an idle tail is correct — scope the safety net to (b)/(c), leave (a) alone.
(4) NEW path (d): BridgeDisconnected (state.rs:465-476) clears context/tokens/activity but NOT steering_queued — a dead bridge leaves the phantom chip too. Make disconnect an explicit drain point of whatever net is chosen.

title: KAS-5 — Host I/O callback responders: fs and terminal (first real cyril-stages interception point)
description: ROADMAP KAS-5 (docs/ROADMAP.md). Depends on KAS-2a (cyril-j16p) — the walking skeleton must render a turn first. Pulled ahead of KAS-3/KAS-4: the first cyril-stages deliverable, converging the KAS track with Phase 2 (ADR-0003). KAS delegates file I/O and shell execution to the host via server->client ACP requests; cyril becomes the executor and therefore the audit/gate/transform point. Scope: implement the HostIo capability sub-trait (the accessor stub pattern is laid down in KAS-0/cyril-atjw) + responders for fs read/write and terminal exec, routed through the single-mediator bridge loop (ADR-0004). INVARIANT INHERITED FROM KAS-0/ADR-0004: the loop forwards requests and NEVER awaits their resolution; cyril-side resolution that is slow (file read / shell exec) spawns OFF-LOOP, the same way the turn prompt does (cyril-84ca). Reintroducing a blocking await in the loop is a regression. Windows/WSL path translation applies at the fs boundary. Filed during KAS-0 budgeted-plan to cite the request-resolution deferral.
notes:
2026-06-30 KAS-5a (fs host-callbacks) COMPLETE on branch feat/kas-5-host-io via gilfoyle flow (prove-it -> falsifiable-design -> budgeted-plan -> checkpointed-build; artifacts in .cyril-7bdu/). 6 slices, 429 tests + clippy -D warnings green, AND the live oracle kas_fs_host_io_smoke PASSED (real KAS turn through cyril's bridge: read+write resolvers served, write permission-gated C9). Build-time re-scope: this issue = fs only; terminal split to cyril-ufie (KAS-5b); ADR-0004 loop-mediation gate seam deferred to cyril-g9vt (direct-resolve in #[cfg(kas)] KiroClient overrides; C4 non-blocking holds via acp per-request spawn + tokio::fs offload). Ready for human review/PR.

Closed: Merged in PR #36 (feat/kas-5-host-io -> main, c89a1f0). fs (5a) + terminal (5b) host-callback responders shipped; review fixes (stdin=null, logged reaps, wedge-on-error, cross-platform test gating) in a128633. CI green on Linux/macOS/Windows. Follow-ups remain as separate issues (3lh8, 0v42, 8tq6, g9vt, r3t6, 1rpv, ihj1).

title: KAS host-io: bounded read for fs/read_text_file (don't load whole file when line/limit set)
description:
kas::host_io::read_text_file uses tokio::fs::read_to_string, loading the ENTIRE file into memory before slice_lines selects [line, line+limit). For a large file with a small limit (KAS's chunked-read pattern), the whole file is read and allocated regardless of how few lines were requested -- a memory spike / OOM risk proportional to file size, not request size.

Consider tokio::io::BufReader + AsyncBufReadExt: skip(start), take(limit), stop early; keep the read-whole path for the None/None case. Must preserve slice_lines' byte-exact semantics (split_inclusive keeps trailing newline; CRLF intact) and the non-UTF-8 -> Err behavior. Per-callback, not a hot loop -- low priority. Discovered during review of PR #34 (KAS-5a).
acceptance_criteria: A read with line/limit on a large file allocates ~O(requested window), not O(file size); slice byte-exactness (trailing newline, CRLF) and non-UTF-8 -> Err preserved; whole-file (None/None) behavior unchanged; existing host_io read tests still pass.
notes:
2026-07-01 issue-set review:
(1) Do NOT use the lines() adapter — it strips the \n AND a trailing \r, destroying the documented byte-exact/CRLF semantics. Correct idiom: manual read_line (or read_until(b'\n')) loop — delimiter included, so \r\n survives; clear a reused buffer for the `start` skipped lines, accumulate `limit` lines, break on Ok(0). O(longest line + window) memory; no meaningfully simpler idiom exists.
(2) One acceptance criterion is unachievable by construction: 'non-UTF-8 -> Err' today validates the WHOLE file (read_to_string); a bounded read cannot validate bytes it never reads. Relax to Err-for-invalid-UTF-8-within-the-read-region and document the divergence (reading to EOF just to validate defeats the issue's purpose).
(3) line-beyond-EOF still yields "" under a streaming loop — matches current behavior, no special case needed.

title: dispatch_steer: optimistic chip+echo leak if bridge.send fails after add_steer_echo
description: app.rs dispatch_steer SteerGate::Send arm calls ui.add_steer_echo(&text) (chip +1, Queued echo committed) BEFORE bridge.send(BridgeCommand::SteerSession).await?. If the channel send errors (bridge thread gone / channel closed), the ? propagates and the function unwinds, but the chip increment and the Queued echo are already committed with no drain path — the steer never reached the backend so no SteeringConsumed/Cleared/Unsupported will arrive. With cyril-7z7u removing the turn-end reset, the chip sticks until /new. Low severity (trigger is a dead bridge, steering already broken) but a real unreconcilable optimistic-UI leak. Fix: either send first and add the echo only on Ok, or roll back (decrement chip + flip the just-added echo to a terminal status) in the Err arm before returning. Found by /code-review of PR 28 (finding #2). app.rs:966.

title: KAS-8 — Surface _kiro/error/rate_limit instead of silently dropping it
description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/error/rate_limit` — an agent->client notification KAS emits when the backend rate-limits a turn. cyril currently drops it via the KAS-2a unknown-variant debug! arm, so the user sees a stalled/empty turn with no explanation.

SCOPE:
- Add a converter arm for `_kiro/error/rate_limit`.
- Surface it as a system message (rate limited — explain + when to retry if the payload carries it); clear on the next successful turn.
- Make sure it interacts correctly with the KAS turn-end / busy-clear path (a rate-limited turn must release the busy guard — see cyril-j16p non-blocking criterion).

REFS: covenant capabilities §1c notifications list (`_kiro/error/rate_limit`); ROADMAP KAS-8 coverage matrix. Probe the live payload shape before implementing.

title: AgentProcess stderr piped but never drained — >64KB cumulative stderr wedges the agent; crash diagnostics discarded
description:
Found by the 2026-07-01 KAS implementation review. AgentProcess::spawn sets .stderr(Stdio::piped()) but child.stderr is never taken or read anywhere (transport.rs:25; verified by grep — _child just holds it). Once the child writes ~64KB (Linux pipe buffer) cumulatively to stderr, its next stderr write BLOCKS the whole process -> the engine wedges mid-turn with no error anywhere (and per cyril-l7tw, the wedge is silent).

Predates KAS but KAS makes it material: the engine is `node --experimental-wasm-modules` (Node emits runtime warnings to stderr by default) running an 801MB TypeScript agent whose logging/tracebacks go to stderr, over sessions that can run for hours. Even below the wedge threshold, everything KAS prints to stderr — including the reason for a spawn-then-immediate-crash — is discarded, so 'ACP initialization failed' is all the user ever gets.

Fix shape: take() stderr and spawn a drain task on the LocalSet (debug!-log lines; ring-buffer the tail so BridgeDisconnected reasons can include it — pairs with cyril-l7tw's visibility fix).
acceptance_criteria: An agent process writing >64KB to stderr does not stall (test with a chatty stub); drained stderr appears at debug level in cyril.log; the last N stderr lines are available to the disconnect path for actionable BridgeDisconnected reasons.

title: Model picker: surface per-model capability (hasEffort + credit rate) from KAS model options
description: KAS model options (session/new configOptions[model].options[]._meta.kiro) carry structured per-model metadata cyril currently DROPS: hasEffort (bool — does the model support thinking-effort levels), rateMultiplier (numeric credit rate), rateUnit, effortSchemaPath. cyril's convert layer discards _meta and CommandOption only has {label,value,description,group,is_current}; hasEffort is consumed nowhere. Goal: parse hasEffort + rateMultiplier from model options and surface in the model picker — a 'supports effort' badge and a credit-cost hint — so the user sees which models are thinking-capable before selecting, and optionally gate the /effort control on the selected model's hasEffort. ENGINE ASYMMETRY (diffed 2026-07-02): hasEffort is KAS-ONLY on the wire. v2 session/new models.availableModels are bare {modelId,name,description}; v2 encodes the rate as a display string in the picker group ('2.20x credits', == KAS rateMultiplier 2.2) and has NO hasEffort — so on v2, effort-capability must keep being inferred from whether /effort is registered in commands/available (2.4.1). So: KAS-first; v2 gets the credit hint via group only. NOTE: showing the CURRENT effort level is ALREADY implemented (toolbar ◇ {effort} from MetadataUpdated.effort) — this issue is about the model-picker capability display, not the current-level indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Model picker shows an effort-capable badge (from KAS hasEffort) + credit-rate hint; /effort optionally gated on selected-model hasEffort (KAS) / commands/available presence (v2); v2 degrades gracefully (no hasEffort on wire).

title: KAS-8 — Surface _kiro/safety/* Infrastructure Safety gate (can block tool calls)
description:
KAS-8 functional gap. `_kiro/safety/{statusChanged,propertiesChanged}` — Infrastructure Safety gate, added in kiro-cli 2.8.0 (embedded @kiro/agent). Cedar/AWS-governance signals that CAN BLOCK A TOOL CALL.

Why this matters more than the other notification gaps: it is ENFORCEMENT, not display. cyril's KAS-2a unknown-variant debug! arm silently drops these today, which means cyril can present a tool as runnable that KAS will refuse, or miss a posture change mid-session. Every other dropped notification just loses information; this one desyncs cyril from what KAS will actually permit.

SCOPE:
- Add a converter arm for `_kiro/safety/statusChanged` + `_kiro/safety/propertiesChanged` (pairs with the KAS-2c GovernanceSource sub-trait — same Cedar/governance family).
- Reflect a safety-blocked tool call in the UI (don't render it as a plain pending/allowed tool).
- Surface the safety status/posture so the user understands why a tool was refused.

REFS: covenant capabilities/safety/*.d.ts (on-disk @kiro/agent 0.3.257; NOT yet in the curated docs/kiro-kas-acp-covenant.md catalog — added after 0.3.224); ROADMAP KAS-8 coverage matrix; memory reference_kiro_2_8_0_diff (notes the safety gate). Probe the live shapes before implementing (no probe exists yet).

title: Remove/align unread SessionController.steering_depth (diverges from optimistic UiState chip)
description:
SessionController.steering_depth (cyril-core/session.rs) is a wire-driven steer counter (SteeringQueued +1, SteeringConsumed -1, Cleared/SessionCreated =0; NO turn-end reset) with ZERO non-test readers (the chip reads UiState.steering_queued via toolbar.rs:80).

cyril-7z7u makes UiState.steering_queued OPTIMISTIC (counted at add_steer_echo / user-send), so it no longer mirrors the wire-driven steering_depth — the "in lockstep with SessionController.steering_depth" comments (state.rs ~538/1909) are now stale. Harmless today (steering_depth is unread), but it's dead/divergent code.

Action: either (a) delete steering_depth + its tests (it's unused), or (b) make it optimistic to match if a future consumer needs a core-side steer count. Decide when touched. Found during cyril-7z7u checkpointed-build.

title: Watch: KAS 0.8.0 new surface _kiro/knowledge/indexing{Started,Completed} + _kiro/sandbox/applyConfig
description: The only net-new _kiro/* methods in @kiro/agent 0.3.299->0.8.0 (73->76, zero removals). Pairs with expanded local-embedding code (Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 feature-extraction pipeline; 'semantic search (MiniLM) or keyword search (BM25)') => local knowledge-base semantic index with progress notifications, plus dynamic sandbox reconfig. No cyril task consumes these today; log as future surface if a knowledge/RAG progress UI or sandbox-control feature is scoped. Re-check each release whether they start arriving unsolicited (would need tolerant ext-notification handling).
acceptance_criteria: Tracked; revisit at next kiro-cli release audit
notes:
LIVE-PROBED 2026-07-02 (probe-kas-knowledge-indexing-2.11.0.py). indexing{Started,Completed} = agent->client extNotifications (fire-and-forget; no client cap needed). Started {sessionId,name,fileCount}; Completed {sessionId,name,status,itemCount?} (itemCount only status='success'). TRIGGER: bind custom agent w/ resources.knowledgeBases[] via set_config_option {configId:'mode', value:'<agentId>'} (custom file agents appear as mode values) -> syncActiveAgentKnowledge at session setup/mode switch. KB schema {type:'knowledgeBase', source:'file://<rel>', name?, description?, indexType?:fast|best, include?, exclude?, autoUpdate?}. KEY: fast=BM25 local completes instantly; best=Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 via @huggingface/transformers allowRemoteModels=true + empty cache -> first use DOWNLOADS from HF hub -> Started fires WITHOUT Completed (>25s). Started-without-Completed is a real state; don't assume terminal Completed (cf KAS-3). _kiro/sandbox/applyConfig also new+confirmed: {configId,value} strings, engine-global no sessionId, unknown value=silent {} no-op, missing value=-32602.

--- CYRIL SCOPING (2026-07-02): NO WIRE ADDITION REQUIRED to be upgrade-safe. Verified in code: (1) KasEngine drops unknown _kiro/* frames to Ok(None) — test kas_engine_drops_unknown_ext_frame (KAS-2a, cyril-j16p); (2) session_info_to_notification (kas.rs) handles only turn_end + context_usage, all other kinds -> _ => None. So both indexing notifications AND steering_queued/cleared drop gracefully; no crash/hang, no client capability handshake change. The knowledge TOOL (agent 'knowledge search') arrives as an ordinary ACP tool_call and already renders. => This issue is PURELY OPTIONAL display work: to show indexing progress, add two receive-side arms in convert_ext_notification for _kiro/knowledge/indexing{Started,Completed} + new internal Notification variants + a UI element (progress must tolerate 3 states: success+itemCount / failed / never-Completed while a ~90MB HF download runs). cyril SENDS nothing for this. _kiro/sandbox/applyConfig = elective client->agent send (mid-session sandbox toggle), likely out of scope. steer/clear tracked separately in cyril-vgcm (K1/KAS-steering track).

--- DESIGN SKETCH (2026-07-02, PROPOSAL — not built). A knowledge progress panel styled like crew_panel (bordered block, cyan ` knowledge ` title, `{icon} ` + bold-white `{name:<20}` + status, `+N more` overflow). One row per base; spinner (SPINNER_CHARS) while between Started/Completed, ✓ green (success)/✗ red (failed) once settled. Each row = one wire event:
  ⠹ downloading -> indexingStarted (best/first-use, ~90MB HF download, no Completed yet)
  ⠋ indexing    -> indexingStarted {fileCount}   (fast/BM25)
  ✓ indexed·Nit -> indexingCompleted status=success itemCount=N
  ✗ failed·rea  -> indexingCompleted status=failed (no itemCount)
80-col ratatui TestBackend render:
  ┌ knowledge ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │⠹ api-docs             downloading model… ~90MB                               │
  │⠋ lighthouse-notes     indexing… 3 files                                      │
  │✓ runbooks             indexed · 128 items                                    │
  │✗ legacy-logs          failed · model load error                              │
  │  +2 more                                                                     │
  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
Render test surfaced two layout facts a mockup hid: (1) verbose status ('… from Hugging Face') CLIPS at <=66 cols -> trim to '~90MB'; (2) at very narrow widths ratatui truncates the status at the inner edge gracefully (no panic/overflow; cf cyril-mdbp). Manual /knowledge path renders as a normal tool_call (⟳/✓/✗), NOT this panel.
DROP-IN PATH: render_knowledge_panel() mirrors crew_panel::render 1:1; to land, swap &[Row] for a &dyn TuiState accessor backed by a KnowledgeTracker (pure state machine, apply_notification pattern like SubagentTracker) fed from the 2 indexing notifications; 5 wire->pixel assertions become the widget test module.
ARTIFACTS: reproducible ratatui render+tests crate at experiments/knowledge-panel-render/ (standalone workspace, ratatui 0.30 only; `cargo run` = 80-col + narrow render, `cargo test` = 5 assertions tying itemCount/failed/~90MB/+N more/cyan-title to the buffer; render_knowledge_panel mirrors crew_panel::render for drop-in). HTML mockup: https://claude.ai/code/artifact/a3baddec-bd9e-4da7-a316-ca271fe94a8a

title: KAS-5b follow-up: honor terminal/create outputByteLimit (truncate at char boundary)
description: KAS-5b (cyril-ufie) ships terminal/output with truncated:false and the full combined output. acp CreateTerminalRequest carries output_byte_limit: Option<u64> (ACP: when output exceeds the limit, the Client truncates from the BEGINNING to stay within it, at a UTF-8 char boundary, setting truncated:true). NOT production-reachable @ kiro-cli 2.10.0 — the host_callbacks_2.10.0.json capture shows KAS sends no outputByteLimit (None). Implement tail-keep truncation + truncated flag when/if a capture shows KAS sending it. Gate: re-probe a KAS release for outputByteLimit on terminal/create before building.

title: K1a steering non-functional: __session/steer double-underscore + dead inbound converter arm
description: prove-it-prototype (cyril-bm1j) against kiro-cli 2.8.0 found K1a steering broken end-to-end in both directions, missed by K1a unit tests (they bypass the real wire). (a) OUTBOUND: bridge.rs:1060/1127 call ExtRequest::new("_session/steer") but agent-client-protocol does format!("_{}",method) (lib.rs:213) -> wire shows __session/steer -> kiro -32601. Fix: pass "session/steer" / "session/steer/clear" (cf. SpawnSession's "session/spawn"). (b) INBOUND: echoes ride wire _kiro.dev/session/update; the lib strips the leading _ (lib.rs:294) so the converter sees kiro.dev/session/update; K1a's arm keyed "_kiro.dev/session/update" (convert/kiro.rs:681) is dead, echoes fall to the existing kiro.dev/session/update arm (line 424) and are dropped. Repro: .k1b-steering/probe.rs + wire_shim.py + oracle-wire-capture.log.
acceptance_criteria: A live mid-turn steer against kiro 2.7.0+ produces SteeringQueued in cyril (no -32601); a captured _kiro.dev/session/update steering echo yields SteeringQueued/Consumed/Cleared. Regression fence: wire-method test asserting the bytes are _session/steer (single _) and a converter test keyed on the stripped method.
notes: Closed: Fixed by PR #20 (merged ad71d4d): outbound bridge.rs now sends unprefixed session/steer[/clear] (single _ on wire); inbound steering echoes folded into the kiro.dev/session/update arm; dead _kiro.dev arm deleted. Verified end-to-end vs kiro 2.8.0 (.k1b-steering/fix-verification.log). Follow-up commit e4ac8e6 added the steer_methods_are_unprefixed regression fence.
